stories.title: Claude Code is steganographically marking requests
stories.titleZh: Claude Code 正在以隐写方式标记请求

comments.author: meowface
comments.textRawHtml:
Value judgment aside: I am a bit surprised at how sloppily they did this. I think they could've achieved the same effect while decreasing the odds of detection via reverse engineering.

(This field is known as "underhanded code", coined by the Underhanded C contest: https://www.underhanded-c.org. It's a little-known "art"; little-known for probably self-explanatory reasons. There are much cleverer ways of achieving objectives like this. One obviously being you can move more out of the client and into the server, but the other being you can write plausibly deniable client code in a much more benign-seeming way than this. Some of what they added can only be done on the client, but I think some could've been moved, and the client-required parts could've been done more subtly and credibly.)

It's possible they knew the JS bundle gets so heavily scrutinized that it'd eventually get spotted and reported on regardless so they didn't bother doing something more subtle and duplicitous. But still seems slightly lazy.
comments.textZhHtml:
抛开价值判断不谈：我对他们这样做的草率感到有点惊讶。我认为他们可以达到相同的效果，同时通过逆向工程降低检测的几率。

（这个领域被称为“卑鄙代码”，由 Underhanded C 竞赛创造：https://www.underhanded-c.org。这是一门鲜为人知的“艺术”；由于可能不言自明的原因而鲜为人知。实现这样的目标有很多更聪明的方法。显然，你可以将更多的东西移出他们添加的一些内容只能在客户端上完成，但我认为有些内容可以被移动，并且客户端所需的部分可以更巧妙、更可信地完成。）没有费心做一些更微妙和口是心非的事情。但还是显得有点懒。

children.author: superfrank
children.textRawHtml:
It's also possible that there are more in-depth detection methods and that this was just a cheap and easy first step that hasn't been removed because it catches a lot of less sophisticated bad actors.

It's unlikely that this will stop a big AI lab from distilling their model if they're really determined, but A) it may be enough to stop a bunch of fly-by-night token resellers looking to make a quick buck and B) you never know when one person at one of those big labs will mess up and forget to install whatever workaround they have and out themselves.

I think of it like if you have a problem with birds in your yard so you go buy one of those plastic owls. The owl scares away most of the birds, but not all of them, so you go and buy some ultrasonic noise thing to scare them away (I'm just making something up). Just because you bought the new ultrasonic thing though, that doesn't mean you're going to take the owl down. You leave it up because now you've got two layers of defense instead of one.
children.textZhHtml:
也有可能存在更深入的检测方法，而这只是一个廉价且简单的第一步，尚未被删除，因为它捕获了许多不那么复杂的坏人。

如果大型人工智能实验室真的下定决心，这不太可能阻止他们提炼他们的模型，但是A）这可能足以阻止一群想要快速赚钱的不可靠的代币经销商，B）你永远不知道什么时候会有一个这些大型实验室之一的人员会搞砸，忘记安装他们拥有的任何解决方法并自行退出。

我认为这就像如果你对院子里的鸟有问题，那么你就去买一只塑料猫头鹰。猫头鹰吓跑了大部分鸟，但不是全部，所以你去买一些超声波噪音的东西来吓跑它们（我只是编造的）。不过，仅仅因为您购买了新的超声波设备，并不意味着您会打倒猫头鹰。您将其保留，因为现在您有两层防御而不是一层。

children.author: meowface
children.textRawHtml: I'm sure they've had complex server-side detections for a while. But for the client parts: it should only contain the parts that must be on the client, and it could be done in a more benign-looking way. For example, the unavoidable client parts could've been done more fuzzily/broadly, for plausible deniability, and then narrowed on the server. (They may already have been following that strategy before now, without being noticed.)
children.textZhHtml: 我确信他们已经进行了复杂的服务器端检测一段时间了。但对于客户端部分：它应该只包含客户端上必须存在的部分，并且可以以一种看起来更良性的方式完成。例如，不可避免的客户端部分可以更加模糊/广泛地完成，以实现合理的否认，然后在服务器上缩小范围。 （他们之前可能已经在遵循该策略，但没有被注意到。）

children.author: weare138
children.textRawHtml: But what's stopping someone from modifying or injecting code into the client to bypass this 'restriction'? This type of security should be implemented server-side and at the network perimeter not in the client, especially one written in TypeScript.
children.textZhHtml: 但是是什么阻止某人修改或向客户端注入代码以绕过此“限制”呢？这种类型的安全性应该在服务器端和网络外围实现，而不是在客户端实现，尤其是用 TypeScript 编写的安全性。

children.textRawHtml:
> I think the models are being optimized for wealth extraction from users and companies, instead of solving problems.

I don't think so. Expect that in a market with high vendor lock-in but that's not the case here. The market is extremely competitive and switching cost are near zero. Anthropic can't afford to pull shit like this and sacrifice quality.

children.author: haspok
children.textRawHtml:
My employer just finalized a contract with Anthropic, for enterprise Claude Code use. Which means that unless there is a _major_ downgrade in service quality, we are now locked in for the next few years (but at least for a year, although vendor contracts are renegotiated less frequently).

Just checked the dashboard, and we seem to have the exact same $200 credit as others, enterprise or not. Token inflation affects us just like everyone else.

It feels a bit like buying the same box of chocolates every day, but the size / weight of the box is shrinking... the price remains unchanged!
children.textZhHtml:
我们公司刚和Anthropic敲定了企业版Claude Code的使用合同。这意味着，除非服务质量出现_重大_下滑，否则我们未来几年都被锁定了（至少锁定一年，尽管供应商合同重新谈判的频率较低）。

刚看了一下后台，我们的额度似乎和其他人一样，都是200美元，不管是不是企业版。Token通胀对我们和其他人一样有影响。

感觉就像每天买同一盒巧克力，但盒子的尺寸/重量却在缩水……价格却一分没降！

children.author: comboy
children.textRawHtml:
You don't have LLM-based processes if you think there is no lock-in. There may be no lock-in for coding if you enforce decent rules (but still some ambiguous docs can be interpreted differently), but any non-trivial pipeline/system, these models are not stupid but each has some quirks. Sometimes for some reason they will ignore some instruction while all other models have no trouble following it. These things accumulate.

Plus there's subjective stuff even for coding, people learning how to deal with it. Even on HN you can already see cloude/codex camps each strongly convinced that one is better than the other.
children.textZhHtml:
如果你觉得没有锁定效应，那是因为你还没用上基于LLM的流程。如果是写代码，只要制定合理的规则，可能确实没有锁定效应（但有些含糊的文档还是会被不同地解读），可一旦涉及任何非平凡的流水线或系统，这些模型虽然不笨，但各有各的怪癖。有时候出于某些原因，它们会无视某些指令，而其他所有模型都能轻松遵循。这些问题会不断累积。

而且即便是写代码也有主观因素，大家都在摸索应对之策。就算在HN上，你也已经能看到Claude和Codex两大阵营，各自坚信自己用的那个更好。

children.author: Terretta
children.textRawHtml:
Anthropic can't afford to pull shit like this and sacrifice quality.

And yet, the Java language exists.

For market share, ANTHROP\C needs to optimize for the vast mediocrity that are mid-bell-curve users and enterprises.

This adaptation tends to come with significant drag for the right ends of the bell curve firms or teams.

Unless ANTHROP\C have a separate objective function by cohort and ensure that doesn't regress, improving results for the emerging middle will nerf tools from point of view of those with high in-domain expertise.
children.textZhHtml:
Anthropic可承受不起搞这种烂事而牺牲质量。

然而，Java语言依然存在。

为了市场份额，ANTHROP\C需要为处于正态分布中间的大量平庸用户和企业进行优化。

这种调整往往会给正态分布右端的企业或团队带来巨大的拖累。

除非ANTHROP\C针对不同群体设定独立的目标函数并确保其不退化，否则为新兴中间群体改善效果，在具备深厚领域专业知识的人看来，就是在削弱工具的性能。

children.author: indoordin0saur
children.textRawHtml: Yeah. Mine really likes to read excess code. I'll ask it questions like "If I move all these three ETL jobs into a subfolder will it break anything?" It'll start with giving me the simple answer but then continue on to consider another question and realize it requires reading my entire other repo that handles all of my cloud's infrastructure. And it'll proceed to read through tens of thousands of lines of terraform.
children.textZhHtml: 是的。我真的很喜欢阅读多余的代码。我会问它这样的问题：“如果我将所有这三个 ETL 作业移到一个子文件夹中，它会破坏任何内容吗？”它首先会给我一个简单的答案，然后继续考虑另一个问题，并意识到它需要阅读我处理所有云基础设施的整个其他存储库。它将继续读取数万行地形。

children.author: anygivnthursday
children.textRawHtml: Then it tells you nothing will break, spend a bunch of tokens on the migration, hit a wall, having an oops moment and telling you it made a mistake assuming a key fact instead of verifying and present you the option to roll everything back or rewrite the rest of your system.
children.textZhHtml: 然后它告诉你什么都不会出错，花了一大堆token进行迁移，结果碰壁了，突然来个“哎呀”时刻，告诉你它犯了个错，因为它假设了一个关键事实而没有去验证，接着给你两个选项：要么全部回滚，要么重写系统的其余部分。

children.author: Terretta
children.textRawHtml:
By contrast, I keep trying to find an incantation that can get it to AT LEAST read the comments surrounding a code target before a grep replace if it isn't going to read more than the first 60 lines of any doc. (Btw, the receiver of a -tail 60 doesn't seem to know it was cut, it insists it "read" the whole file.)

It seems to me ANTHROP\C have harnessed hard for not spending tokens to bring content into context. I wish they'd left us a "LEROY_JENKINS" flag: read and think before you code. In Claude Code anyway, it appears to default to:

  export CLAUDE_CODE_LEROY_JENKINS=true
children.textZhHtml:
相比之下，我一直在试图找到一种“咒语”，能让它至少在执行 grep 替换前，先读一下代码目标周围的注释，哪怕它不打算读任何文档前60行之后的内容。（顺便说一句，接收 `-tail 60` 输出的家伙似乎不知道内容被截断了，它坚称自己“读”了整个文件。）

在我看来，ANTHROP\C 似乎把hard用在了不消耗 token 将内容纳入上下文上。我真希望他们能给我们留一个“LEROY_JENKINS”标志：先读先想，再写代码。至少在 Claude Code 中，它似乎默认设置为：

  export CLAUDE_CODE_LEROY_JENKINS=true

children.author: post-it
children.textRawHtml:
> I don't know why Opus would try to create an entire library when I told it specifically to do something simple that would take 2-3 lines of Python.

Because it reasons in one direction. First it encounters some kind of issue with 2-3 lines of Python that might make it not work, and then it goes onto plan B, which is making a library, but it doesn't circle back and compare the effort of making the library to working around whatever might make the 2-3 lines not work. Except sometimes it does, because it's inscrutable.
children.textZhHtml:
> 当我明确告诉 Opus 做一些简单的事情（需要 2-3 行 Python 代码）时，我不知道为什么 Opus 会尝试创建整个库。

因为它在一个方向上推理。首先，它遇到了 2-3 行 Python 的某种问题，可能会使其无法工作，然后它进入 B 计划，即创建一个库，但它不会回过头来比较创建库的工作量，以解决可能导致 2-3 行无法工作的任何问题。但有时确实如此，因为它难以理解。

children.author: ethbr1
children.textRawHtml: So, as the quip goes, junior level reasoning.
children.textZhHtml: 所以，正如那句俏皮话所说，这是初级水平的推理。

children.author: Traubenfuchs
children.textRawHtml: It's really bad when you let opus do investigations on broken java or infrastructure stuff. It starts decompiling .jar, sometimes multiple versions of the same dependency, reading every single kubernetes/terraform file and loading all the logs and info kubectl offers.
children.textZhHtml: 让 opus 去排查 Java 或基础设施的故障，那场面简直糟透了。它会开始反编译 .jar 文件，有时甚至是同一个依赖的多个版本，读取每一个 Kubernetes/Terraform 文件，并加载 kubectl 提供的所有日志和信息。

children.author: anygivnthursday

children.textZhHtml: 这是新出的功能吗？至少我最近才注意到，它现在不知为何不再直接查看源码，而是倾向于反编译并读取Java字节码。

children.author: benny_s
children.textRawHtml: My experience with Opus in the last weeks is the opposite. I have the feeling Opus got smarter since they released and blocked Fable. Maybe they got more compute available since a) they finished Training Mythos/Fable and b) couldn't provide inference for it?
children.textZhHtml: 我过去几周对 Opus 的体验正好相反。我有一种感觉，自从 Opus 发布并屏蔽了《神鬼寓言》之后，他们变得更加聪明了。也许他们获得了更多可用计算，因为 a) 他们完成了神话/寓言训练，b) 无法为其提供推理？

stories.title: Qwen 3.6 27B is the sweet spot for local development
stories.titleZh: Qwen 3.6 27B是本地开发的最佳选择

comments.author: iagooar
comments.textRawHtml:
I love my MacBook Pro M5 128GB RAM and I love qwen3.6.

BUT DO NOT buy this MacBook if you plan on doing serious coding using local LLMs with it. The reason is simple: your fingers will burn and your head will explode from the noise.

Running any kind of sophisticated job on the very laptop you are using is just not viable. Sure you can use it in clamshell mode, but forget touching it while working with AI coding or agents.

If you want to run Qwen3.6 27B / 35B at its best, get a MacMini M4 with 64GB of RAM and put it in the basement - or at least a few meters from your desk. Connect to it over LAN or Tailscale. The MacMini will also cost you almost 1/3 of the MacBook Pro.

Thank me later.
comments.textZhHtml:
我喜欢我的 MacBook Pro M5 128GB RAM，也喜欢 qwen3.6。

但如果您打算使用本地法学硕士进行认真的编码，请不要购买这款 MacBook。原因很简单：您的手指会被灼伤，您的头会因噪音而爆炸。

在您正在使用的笔记本电脑上运行任何复杂的工作都是不可行的。当然，您可以在翻盖模式下使用它，但在使用 AI 编码或代理时忘记触摸它。

如果您想以最佳状态运行 Qwen3.6 27B / 35B，请购买一台具有 64GB RAM 的 MacMini M4，并将其放在地下室 - 或至少距离办公桌几米的地方。通过 LAN 或 Tailscale 连接到它。 MacMini 的价格几乎是 MacBook Pro 的 1/3。

稍后谢谢我。

children.author: jasonjmcghee
children.textRawHtml:
I'm surprised no one has else has mentioned - low power mode.

With no speculative decoding, using high power mode, I get 80 t/s on 35B A3B - and it gets hot and spins up. On low power mode I get 38 t/s - no fans, cool to warm laptop.

If you currently don't use speculative decoding and you start using it, it can nearly offset the difference between high and low power, and it's night and day experience.

I almost always keep my laptop on low power mode.
children.textZhHtml:
我很惊讶没有人提到 - 低功耗模式。

在没有推测解码的情况下，使用高功率模式，我在 35B A3B 上得到 80 t/s - 并且它变热并旋转。在低功耗模式下，我的速度为 38 t/s - 无风扇，从冷到热的笔记本电脑。

如果您当前不使用推测解码并且开始使用它，它几乎可以抵消高功率和低功率之间的差异，并且是夜间和白天的体验。

我几乎总是将笔记本电脑保持在低功耗模式。

children.author: Lwerewolf
children.textRawHtml: Same with ds4-flash. Low power is 13t/s vs 26t/s, power usage is ~30w vs ~100-120w. I still use high power because my m5 max is basically a server with built-in UPS and screen (and an off-work media machine, blender cycles toy, etc - growing fond of it, actually), but even 10tok/s is usable with a relatively smart model. Caveat is that the code that I touch barely has any boilerplate and  I like keeping it lean.
children.textZhHtml: 与 ds4-flash 相同。低功耗为 13t/s vs 26t/s，功耗约为 30w vs ~100-120w。我仍然使用高功率，因为​​我的 m5 max 基本上是一个带有内置 UPS 和屏幕的服务器（还有一个下班媒体机、搅拌机循环玩具等 - 实际上越来越喜欢它），但即使是 10tok/s 也可以用于相对智能的型号。需要注意的是，我接触的代码几乎没有任何样板，我喜欢保持精简。

children.author: html5cat
children.textRawHtml: Awesome idea! Will try it out. Wish there was a way to enable low power on a per-app basis. Scrolling and reading on low power mode is really annoying.
children.textZhHtml: 很棒的主意！会尝试一下。希望有一种方法可以在每个应用程序的基础上启用低功耗。在低功耗模式下滚动和阅读确实很烦人。

children.author: the_lucifer
children.textRawHtml:
> Wish there was a way to enable low power on a per-app basis.

Since you can control the low power mode setting from the command line: `sudo pmset -a lowpowermode 1`.

It should be pretty straightforward to hook this up to Hammerspoon[1] using hs.application.frontmostApplication() to apply the setting based on whatever foreground application you choose.

Thinking out loud, that being said, the necessity of sudo might make this slightly more complex. An always on background admin agent might be needed I suppose to bypass the password prompts (or add pmset to the sudoers file, if you prefer).

[1]: https://www.hammerspoon.org/
children.textZhHtml:
> 希望能有一种方法可以按应用启用低功耗模式。

因为你可以通过命令行控制低功耗模式设置：`sudo pmset -a lowpowermode 1`。

使用hs.application.frontmostApplication()将其与Hammerspoon[1]连接起来，根据你选择的任何前台应用来应用该设置，应该是非常直接的。

顺便说一下，不过，需要sudo权限可能会让这稍微复杂一点。我想可能需要一个始终在后台运行的管理员代理来绕过密码提示（或者如果你愿意，可以将pmset添加到sudoers文件中）。

[1]: https://www.hammerspoon.org/

children.author: kmacdough
children.textRawHtml: Unfortunately doesn't cover scrolling HN while the agent toils away.
children.textZhHtml: 遗憾的是，它没有涵盖在智能体辛勤工作时滚动HN的场景。

children.author: jasonjmcghee
children.textRawHtml:
I usually get mlx versions of models and didn't realize I was using non-mlx - misattributing the performance boost to speculative decoding.

With dflash-mlx library, with dflash disabled 3.6-35b-a3b mlx model I'm getting ~60 t/s on low power and >100 t/s on high power.

Compared with previous message being official qwen huggingface release (non-mlx) using lm studio.
children.textZhHtml:
我通常会获得 mlx 版本的模型，但没有意识到我使用的是非 mlx - 将性能提升错误地归因于推测解码。

使用 dflash-mlx 库，在 dflash disabled 3.6-35b-a3b mlx 模型中，我在低功率下获得约 60 t/s，在高功率下获得 >100 t/s力量。

与之前使用 lm studio 发布的官方 qwen Huggingface 版本（非 mlx）的消息相比。

children.author: anon373839
children.textRawHtml: Can you mention what inference stack you're using? I've tried MTP several times with that model and it always seems to significantly cut my token generation speed from ~60 tokens/sec to ~40 (M3 Max).
children.textZhHtml: 您能提及您正在使用的推理堆栈吗？我已经多次尝试使用该模型进行 MTP，它似乎总是将我的代币生成速度从约 60 个代币/秒显着降低到约 40 个代币/秒（M3 Max）。

children.author: jasonjmcghee
children.textRawHtml: (see above reply to myself) I misattributed the gain from dflash - it was dflash-mlx library + mlx model, not dflash itself giving me the speedup.
children.textZhHtml: （见上面我给自己的回复）我错误地归因了dflash带来的提升——实际上是dflash-mlx库加上mlx模型，而不是dflash本身给我带来了速度提升。

children.author: c16
children.textRawHtml: Will give this a try later. Enjoy working with A3B Coder, but the heat coming out my 32gb M5 is a lot. This might be the trick - Thanks!
children.textZhHtml: 稍后会尝试一下。喜欢使用 A3B Coder，但我的 32gb M5 发出的热量很大。这可能是窍门 - 谢谢！

children.author: c16
children.textRawHtml: Update: This works super well. Thanks.
children.textZhHtml: 更新：这个方法效果非常好。谢谢。

children.author: mycall
children.textRawHtml: It is less efficient use of the GPU and uses more electricity overall, no?
children.textZhHtml: 这样对GPU的利用效率更低，而且总体上更耗电，不是吗？

children.author: spider-mario
children.textRawHtml: Oh no, 0.6 kWh a day!
children.textZhHtml: 哦不，一天0.6度电！

children.author: bigyabai
children.textRawHtml: Yes, this is a tradeoff that foregoes the efficiency of race-to-idle.
children.textZhHtml: 是的，这是一种权衡，它放弃了“竞速至空闲”策略的效率。

children.author: astrostl
children.textRawHtml:
> MacBook Pro M5 128GB RAM

614 GB/s of memory bandwidth

> MacMini M4 with 64GB of RAM

273 GB/s of memory bandwidth (also only currently available with 48GB)

When it comes to inference speed, you want your model to fit in memory, and then to have as much memory bandwidth as possible. In this case a hypothetical Mini with 1TB of memory would still be over 2x slower with 27-35B models.

And FWIW I have an M4 Max MBP 128GB that I keep on a Roost laptop stand, with a separate keyboard/mouse/video. It does fire up the cooling jets when running local LLMs, but stays within tolerance for me on noise. I haven't heat-tested it on longer runs, but I imagine the risen airflow helps a ton.
children.textZhHtml:
> MacBook Pro M5 128GB RAM

614 GB/s 内存带宽

> MacMini M4，配备 64GB RAM

273 GB/s 内存带宽（目前也仅提供 48GB）

在推理速度方面，您希望模型适合内存，然后拥有尽可能多的内存带宽。在这种情况下，假设具有 1TB 内存的 Mini 对于 27-35B 型号的速度仍然会慢 2 倍以上。

FWIW 我有一个 M4 Max MBP 128GB，我把它放在 Roost 笔记本电脑支架上，并配有单独的键盘/鼠标/视频。当运行本地法学硕士时，它确实会启动冷却喷嘴，但噪音仍处于我的容忍范围内。我还没有对它进行长时间的热测试，但我想上升的气流有很大帮助。

children.author: bigyabai
children.textRawHtml:
> When it comes to inference speed, you want your model to fit in memory, and then to have as much memory bandwidth as possible.

This is only true when your GPU isn't bottlenecked building a KV cache, which it usually will be on Apple Silicon. The Achilles heel of the M-series chips are their weak, SOC-grade GPU that holds back the Max and Ultra models from having interactive TTFTs on larger models and contexts.
children.textZhHtml:
> 谈到推理速度，你希望模型能完全放入内存，然后拥有尽可能大的内存带宽。

这只有在你的GPU在构建KV缓存时没有遇到瓶颈的情况下才成立，而在苹果芯片上通常都会遇到瓶颈。M系列芯片的致命弱点在于其较弱的、SoC级别的GPU，这限制了Max和Ultra型号在更大模型和上下文场景中实现交互式TTFT。

children.author: fancyfredbot
children.textRawHtml: Normally people refer to the compute-bound phase as "prefill". Nothing wrong with saying it's building the kv cache though, it's accurate just unusual.
children.textZhHtml: 通常人们将计算密集型阶段称为“预填充”。不过，说它是在构建KV缓存也没错，这种说法很准确，只是不太常见。

children.author: iagooar
children.textRawHtml: On paper the M4 should be roughly 1/3 of the M5, in practice it is only 1/2. With the right, optimized model like qwen3.6 35B MoE MLX you can get over 40 tok / sec on it. I run dozens of background jobs that are not time-critical on it.
children.textZhHtml: 理论上M4的性能应该大约是M5的三分之一，但实际上只有一半。如果使用合适的优化模型，比如qwen3.6 35B MoE MLX，你可以达到每秒40多个token。我在上面运行着几十个对时间要求不高的后台任务。

children.author: bfjvibybd6cuvu6
children.textRawHtml: What kind of jobs?
children.textZhHtml: 什么类型的工作？

children.author: iagooar
children.textRawHtml: Scanning mailbox, reading and classifying emails. Scanning a knowledge base, reading and improving individual articles, reading support interactions and creating summaries, checking and researching new leads that signed up. So much that is possible.
children.textZhHtml: 扫描邮箱，阅读和分类邮件。扫描知识库，阅读和改进单篇文章，阅读客服交互记录并生成摘要，检查和调研新注册的潜在客户。能做的事情太多了。

stories.title: Department of Commerce has lifted export controls on Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5
stories.titleZh: 美国商务部取消了《克劳德寓言5》和《神话5》的出口管制

comments.author: digitaltrees
comments.textRawHtml:
I am migrating my company to private open source AI and building custom tooling, orchestration, an IDE and harness around it. The commercial labs have show it’s far too risky to build on top of them.

With the labs moving into the app layer every interaction with the API related to product development or innovation is data they will steal and use to compete against you by adding those features to their everything apps. No one would hire a knowledge firm (law firm, accountants, management consultants) if they could steal your information and give it to your competitors. Their actions have remained me that we can’t tolerate that with AI systems.

With spaceX buying cursor and Google buying windsurf, and gathering all the developer interaction as training data, there is too much risk that development process itself will be rug pulled.
comments.textZhHtml:
我正在将我的公司迁移到私有开源 AI，并围绕它构建自定义工具、编排、IDE 和工具。商业实验室已经表明，在它们之上进行构建风险太大。

随着实验室进入应用程序层，与产品开发或创新相关的 API 的每一次交互都是他们将窃取的数据，并通过将这些功能添加到他们的所有应用程序中来与您竞争。如果知识公司（律师事务所、会计师、管理顾问）能够窃取您的信息并将其提供给您的竞争对手，那么没有人会雇用他们。他们的行为一直让我觉得我们不能容忍人工智能系统出现这种情况。

随着 SpaceX 购买 Cursor 和 Google 购买 Windsurf，并将所有开发人员交互收集为训练数据，开发过程本身被拖垮的风险太大了。

children.author: aspenmartin
children.textRawHtml:
This makes sense but I am not comfortable with the open source picture either. It depends on the use case and long term strategy. If you're handling customer support tickets or something, beyond some capability I can't imagine the ROI would be worth needing the absolute frontier. You are then in a wonderfully lovely place: absolutely full control over the model and inference stack (if you want).

But

- Plenty of businesses are stuck between a rock and a hard place: make 3p models load bearing, or sacrifice performance to the competitors that are willing to swallow that risk

- I just cannot imagine a viable equilibrium where OSS models compete on capabilities with the frontier without a hidden payer and shaky economics. Quant funds, some sovereign AI effort, cloud business, sanctioned distilled frontier models; all of these are demonstrably viable vehicles for OSS development. But the optimal position for these purposes is not to be the best, it's to be good enough (which I think is the position they find themselves in). I can imagine temporary points where open models pull ahead but not sustainably.

I agree there are many many use cases where the optimal choice is to reach for open weight models (I assume yours is one of them).

But the economics of frontier model development necessitates that these models are behind and thats a problem for other cases.
children.textZhHtml:
这是有道理的，但我对开源图片也不满意。这取决于用例和长期策略。如果您正在处理客户支持票证或其他东西，超出某些能力，我无法想象投资回报率是否值得需要绝对前沿。然后你就处于一个非常可爱的地方：完全控制模型和推理堆栈（如果你愿意的话）。

但是

-很多企业陷入进退两难的境地：让 3p 模型承载，或者牺牲性能给愿意承担这种风险的竞争对手

-我无法想象一种可行的平衡，在这种平衡中，OSS 模型在没有隐藏支付者和不稳定经济的情况下与前沿技术进行能力竞争。量化基金、一些主权人工智能项目、云业务、经认可的前沿模型；所有这些都是开放源码软件开发的明显可行的工具。但实现这些目的的最佳位置不是最好，而是足够好（我认为这就是他们所处的位置）。我可以想象开放模型领先但不可持续的临时点。

我同意在很多用例中，最佳选择是采用开放权重模型（我假设你的模型就是其中之一）。

但是前沿模型开发的经济学要求这些模型落后，这对于其他情况来说是一个问题。

children.author: digitaltrees
children.textRawHtml:
I am not sure I understand your arguments. You seem to be taking both positions. I suspect that is because there are a few confounding variables and diverse needs AI solves and that makes a cohesive analysis hard to reach. I have a clear view that cuts through the complexity, whether you find it useful or persuasive is another matter but I’ll lay it out.

1. All intelligence has value. A Harvard MBA or Stanford CS engineer have value. Haiku has value, Opus has value.

2. Sometimes it makes sense to buy, build or rent capabilities. But lack of ownership of something your business requires to operate is an existential risk which requires ownership.

3. Current OSS models are good enough for substantial automation and productivity gains with the right orchestration and harness. Especially with domain experts in the loop.

4. Frontier models while better, introduce existential risk; so they are a temptation that should be ignored if you care about your business being able to continue to exist.

Let’s invert this conclusion. Imagine you build a workflow around Fable 5 and the workflow going down would harm your business. Well the government has demonstrated ability and willingness to impose a multi day / week / month interruption in your business with no legally required notice, right to appeal or compensation. And further demonstrates a willingness to give preferential access to your competitors.

For these reasons, the only viable path is a model you have on servers you control and can replace if needed. That means cloud providers are fine if you have the model archived somewhere.
children.textZhHtml:
我不确定我是否理解您的论点。你似乎同时担任这两个职位。我怀疑这是因为人工智能解决了一些令人困惑的变量和多样化的需求这使得很难进行有凝聚力的分析。我有一个清晰的观点，可以消除复杂性，你是否觉得它有用或有说服力是另一回事，但我会列出来。

1.所有的智力都有价值。哈佛 MBA 或斯坦福计算机科学工程师很有价值。俳句有价值，作品有价值。

2.有时购买、构建或租赁功能是有意义的。但是，缺乏对企业运营所需的东西的所有权是一种存在风险，需要所有权。

3.当前的 OSS 模型足以通过正确的编排和利用来大幅提高自动化和生产力。尤其是领域专家参与其中。

4。前沿模型虽然更好，但引入了存在风险；因此，如果您关心自己的企业能否继续存在，那么就应该忽略它们的诱惑。

让我们反转这个结论。想象一下，您围绕《神鬼寓言 5》构建了一个工作流程，而该工作流程的崩溃会损害您的业务。政府已经表现出了能力和意愿，可以在没有法律要求的通知、上诉权或赔偿的情况下对您的业务实施多天/周/月的中断。并进一步表明愿意为竞争对手提供优先访问权限。

出于这些原因，唯一可行的途径是您在自己控制的服务器上拥有一个模型，并且可以在需要时进行替换。这意味着如果您将模型存档在某个地方，云提供商就可以了。

children.author: aspenmartin
children.textRawHtml:
I agree with everything you're saying. And I would also agree if you can run your business adequately on OSS models you should absolutely do that for exactly the reasons you say. All I mean is: frontier models will always have their place and I don't see a way for OSS to ever change that picture. I also worry that the economics that makes meaningful open weights model development possible may break someday but I don't really see a mechanism for this at this point.

I don't actually have a use case in mind where you would _need_ or get a substantial competitive advantage of the absolute frontier in some sort of workflow or whatever load bearing DAG there is for your business. But if there are cases like this, that will suck.
children.textZhHtml:
我同意你所说的一切。我也同意，如果您可以在 OSS 模型上充分运行您的业务，那么您绝对应该这样做，原因正是您所说的。我的意思是：前沿模型将永远占有一席之地，而且我认为 OSS 无法改变这种情况。我还担心，使有意义的开放权重模型开发成为可能的经济学可能有一天会被打破，但目前我还没有真正看到这种机制。

我实际上并没有想到一个用例，您需要或在某种工作流程或任何适合您的业务的承载 DAG 中获得绝对前沿的实质性竞争优势。但如果有有这样的情况，那就糟糕了。

children.author: digitaltrees
children.textRawHtml:
Red hat did it for Linux. Someone needs to do the same for AI. Make money on the implementation and maintenance not the api tokens. Tech companies have gotten fat and Happy on recurring revenue. But they are abusing the trust users have placed in them. I feel a back lash in a way I’ve never experienced.

I think AI labs are assuming that if they build and serve intelligence every one will want it. I think the last month has resulted in a lot of people saying “f u I would rather fail in life than serve you”. I personally went from feeling excited and optimistic about building amazing things on top of the models to hell bent on being self reliant and lots of my friends are doing the same.
children.textZhHtml:
红帽为Linux做到了这一点。现在需要有人为AI做同样的事。靠实施和维护赚钱，而不是靠API令牌。科技公司靠着经常性收入赚得盆满钵满、心满意足。但他们正在滥用用户赋予他们的信任。我感受到了一种前所未有的强烈反弹。

我认为AI实验室假设，只要他们构建并提供智能，每个人都会想要它。我认为过去一个月让很多人开始说“去你的，我宁愿人生失败也不愿为你服务”。我个人从最初对在模型之上构建出色产品感到兴奋和乐观，转变为铁了心要自力更生，我的许多朋友也是如此。

children.author: aspenmartin
children.textRawHtml:
I think the problem is whether you're for or against local models, the training strategies etc are roughly the same.

> I think the last month has resulted in a lot of people saying “f u I would rather fail in life than serve you”.

I agree a lot of people are saying this and doing this but this won't last long. People do not want to suffer or fall behind their peers. At some point not using AI for anything will not be a feasible option. Unless you operate from an incredibly uncommon and privileged financial position, you don't really even have a choice.
children.textZhHtml:
我认为问题在于，无论你是否支持本地模型，其训练策略等都大致相同。

> 我认为过去一个月让很多人开始说“去你的，我宁愿人生失败也不愿为你服务”。

我同意很多人正在这么说、这么做，但这不会持续太久。人们不想受苦，也不想落后于同龄人。在某个节点，完全不用AI将不再是一个可行的选择。除非你处于极其罕见且优越的财务地位，否则你其实根本没有选择。

children.author: digitaltrees
children.textRawHtml:

You are right that the temptation to use the asymmetric advantage AI gives will be intense. But banks felt that temptation in 2008 in with cheap capital and 40x leverage. Some banks resisted. Which ones do you think survived in 2010? Business and technology moves in cycles. Optimize for longevity and let your competitors go out of business
children.textZhHtml:

comments.author: nlh
comments.textRawHtml:
Here's a copy of the letter that Commerce sent to Anthropic (note who it'a NOT addressed to...)

Source: https://x.com/AndrewCurran_/status/2072103733715194048?s=20

-------

June 30, 2026

Tom Brown Chief Compute Officer Anthropic 548 Market Street San Francisco, CA 94104

Dear Mr. Brown:

Since the issuance of my previous letters, dated June 12, 2026 and June 26, 2026, Anthropic has taken steps in close coordination with the U.S. government to address the risks associated with Claude Mythos 5 and Claude Fable 5. Among other things, Anthropic has agreed to proactively detect and address security risks associated with the models; to work diligently with the U.S. government on protocols and standards and releases for Mythos, Fable, and future models; and to inform the U.S. government of any malicious activity.

In light of these actions and commitments, as well as the Bureau of Industry and Security's evaluation of the diversion risks now presented by Claude Mythos 5 and Claude Fable 5, the controls in the June 12 letter are withdrawn. A license is no longer required for the export, reexport, or in-country transfer, including deemed export or deemed reexport, of the Mythos or Fable models.

Commerce reserves the right to reevaluate the decisions made in this letter and the necessity of reimposing a license requirement, should circumstances change or should Anthropic fail to adhere to its commitments.

If you have any questions about this letter, please contact me or the Under Secretary of Commerce for Industry and Security, Jeffrey Kessler, at (202) 255-1864.

Sincerely,

Howard W. Lutnick

------

children.author: II2II
children.textRawHtml:
> They must feel free to do as they please since they know consumers are trapped.

My take is somewhat difference: Sony is offloading the cost of their prior decisions onto consumers.

For things like movies, they should have negotiated a contract where sold copies are sold copies and cannot be revoked (even if their right to sell/rent copies lapse). For things like the PS3 store, it cannot be run indefinitely. That said, from my understanding, the authorization keys expire if the clock battery on the PS3 dies. That should not be permitted.

I don't think that this is a "do as they please" situation. I think it is a case of bad decisions being made in the past. For some, like the movies, there isn't much they can do to fix the problem after the fact. There is absolutely no incentive for the rights holders to let consumers continue to access previously purchased content (especially with Sony taking all of the blame). Even something like offering refunds to people who purchased the movies is problematic. In all probability, all of their contracts have similar terms. They would have to refund everyone for every purchase in the long run.

Other stuff, like access to PS3 purchases, are likely fixable. The question is: where is the incentive? They could create a patch for old consoles, but it would only affect a small number of customers who still have those consoles. (Worse yet, it wouldn't do anything for those who stored their consoles in the closet -- only to pull it out later to discover the authorization keys are invalid.) The math probably doesn't work out for them so they aren't going to do it.
children.textZhHtml:
> 他们必须随心所欲，因为他们知道消费者被困住了。

我的看法有些不同：索尼正在将他们之前决策的成本转嫁到消费者身上。

对于电影之类的东西，他们应该协商一份合同，其中所售副本就是已售副本且不能撤销（即使他们出售/租赁副本的权利失效）。对于像 PS3 商店这样的东西，它不能无限期地运行。也就是说，根据我的理解，如果 PS3 上的时钟电池没电，授权密钥就会过期。这是不应该被允许的。

我不认为这是一种“为所欲为”的情况。我认为这是过去做出错误决定的一个例子。对于某些人来说，就像电影中的那样，事后他们无能为力解决问题。权利持有者绝对没有动力让消费者继续访问以前购买的内容（尤其是索尼承担了所有责任）。甚至向购买电影的人提供退款之类的事情也是有问题的。他们的所有合同很可能都有相似的条款。从长远来看，他们必须向每个人的每次购买退款。

其他问题，例如购买 PS3 的权限，可能是可以修复的。问题是：动力在哪里？他们可以为旧控制台创建补丁，但这只会影响仍然拥有这些控制台的少数客户。 （更糟糕的是，对于那些将控制台存放在壁橱里的人来说，它没有任何作用 - 只是后来将其拉出来才发现授权密钥无效。）数学可能不适合他们，所以他们不会这样做。

The trouble with that is you cannot operate a business where you pay your suppliers for a product then give everything away.

I am saying that as a reflection of reality, not to absolve Sony. Someone at Sony must have understood that their licensing agreements were incompatible with the definition of sale. Someone else likely stuck some clauses into the EULA to reflect that, fully realizing that no-one reads those things (also realizing that it is not reasonable to expect consumers to read an EULA for every transaction in their life). The someone who is now responsible for executing the outcome would also understand that there is the potential for legal action on such a matter, but they also understand that there are legal machinations that will, at worse, limit the damages to a sum that is lower than the cost of refunding the full value of each and every purchase made through their service.

stories.title: Android Developer Verification: Threat masquerading as protection
stories.titleZh: Android开发者验证：伪装成防护的威胁

comments.author: sambuccid
comments.textRawHtml:
It doesn't solve the current issue, but in case we don't manage to push back on this, some people might not know that there are various actual linux OSes for mobile:

- SailfishOS: still linux based and seems fairly community inclusive, but the UI part of the stack is closed source. Is the only one officially allowed to run android apps, via emulation. Has existed for a very long time, it's lightweight and I think the most stable/bug-free in this list.

- Ubuntu Touch: fully open source and community driven, it uses snap packages for security, you might be able to run android apps. Last time I run it also seemed fairly stable/bug-free.

- PureOS: fully open source and privacy focused. I think it's the only one that, released with the Librem 5, can avoid using proprietary blobs for interfacing with the hardware. Seems less stable than SailfishOS and Ubuntu Touch. You would need to buy a fairly expensive-but-old phone(librem 5) to run it.

- PostmarketOS: fully open source, focused on being lightweight and revive old phones, has a huge amount of phones it has been tested on, is based on Alpine.

- Mobian: mobile version of Debian, it's fairly new on this list.

There are many more linux mobile OSes, but as far as I know these are the main ones. There might also be some inaccuracies on this post, I tested some of these a long time ago, and I never actually run the last 2.
comments.textZhHtml:
它并不能解决当前的问题，但万一我们无法对此进行反击，有些人可能不知道有各种实际的移动 Linux 操作系统：

- SailfishOS：仍然基于 Linux，似乎具有相当的社区包容性，但堆栈的 UI 部分是闭源的。是唯一官方允许通过模拟运行 Android 应用程序的应用程序。已经存在很长时间了，它很轻量，我认为是这个列表中最稳定/无错误的。

- Ubuntu Touch：完全开源和社区驱动，它使用 snap 包来保证安全，你也许能够运行 Android 应用程序。上次我运行它时看起来也相当稳定/无错误。

- PureOS：完全开源且注重隐私。我认为它是唯一一个与 Librem 5 一起发布的、可以避免使用专有 blob 与硬件连接的产品。似乎不如 SailfishOS 和 Ubuntu Touch 稳定。你需要购买一部相当昂贵但旧的手机（librem 5）来运行它。

- PostmarketOS：完全开源，专注于轻量级和复兴旧手机，有大量手机经过测试，基于 Alpine。

- Mobian：Debian 的移动版本，在这个列表中相当新。

还有更多的 linux 移动操作系统，但据我所知这些是主要的。这篇文章可能也有一些不准确的地方，我很久以前就测试过其中一些，但我从未真正运行过最后两个。

children.author: grapheneos
children.textRawHtml:
> It doesn't solve the current issue

These operating systems aren't compatible with most of the apps and services people want to use. It's going to become much worse. The compatibility layers several provide have extremely poor compatibility combined with disabling the Android security model and app sandbox. Apps running in those compatibility layers are much less contained with less isolation from the Linux kernel, not more.

Aside from that, many people care about privacy and security. Each of those operating systems is far less private and drastically less secure than the Android Open Source Project. None has a truly complete and working app sandbox or permission model. None uses modern exploit protections. None has serious hardware-based encryption features needed to protect against data extraction. They're not serious alternatives to an iPhone from a privacy and security perspective as an AOSP-based OS on decent hardware can be.

> but in case we don't manage to push back on this

It's a warning that's being added to Google Mobile Services operating systems. It doesn't negatively impact other operating systems based on the Android Open Source Project.

> various actual linux OSes for mobile

Linux doesn't mean GNU/Linux or systemd/Linux. It doesn't at all imply using glibc, systemd, GNU coreutils, Bash, GNOME, etc. Distributions using different userspace components including several of the ones you've listed are still Linux Android-based operating systems including AOSP and GrapheneOS are Linux distributions. Alpine doesn't use glibc and SailfishOS has a lot of their own mix of open and closed source software. Using a typical desktop Linux userspace stack isn't what makes it Linux and there's also not a lot of consistency in what's used on desktops regardless. A Linux distribution not using musl, glibc, GNU coreutils, etc. is still Linux.

> There are many more linux mobile OSes, but as far as I know these are the main ones. There might also be some inaccuracies on this post, I tested some of these a long time ago, and I never actually run the last 2.

AOSP-based mobile operating systems are Linux distributions.
children.textZhHtml:
> 它不能解决当前问题

这些操作系统与人们想要使用的大多数应用程序和服务不兼容。情况会变得更糟。几个提供的兼容性层的兼容性极差，并且禁用了 Android 安全模型和应用程序沙箱。在这些兼容层中运行的应用程序受到的限制要少得多，与 Linux 内核的隔离也更少，而不是更多。

除此之外，许多人都关心隐私和安全。这些操作系统的私密性和安全性都远不如 Android 开源项目。没有一个具有真正完整且有效的应用程序沙箱或权限模型。没有一个使用现代的漏洞保护。没有一个具有严格的基于硬件的加密功能来防止数据提取。从隐私和安全的角度来看，它们并不是 iPhone 的严肃替代品，而在体面的硬件上基于 AOSP 的操作系统则可以做到这一点。

> 但如果我们无法阻止这一点

这是一个正在添加到 Google 移动服务操作系统中的警告。它不会对基于 Android 开源项目的其他操作系统产生负面影响。

>各种实际的移动 Linux 操作系统

Linux 并不意味着 GNU/Linux 或 systemd/Linux。它根本不意味着使用 glibc、systemd、GNU coreutils、Bash、GNOME 等。使用不同用户空间组件（包括您列出的几个组件）的发行版仍然是基于 Linux Android 的操作系统，包括 AOSP 和 GrapheneOS 都是 Linux 发行版。 Alpine 不使用 glibc，而 SailfishOS 有很多自己的开源和闭源软件组合。使用典型的桌面 Linux 用户空间堆栈并不是 Linux 的本质，而且桌面上使用的内容也没有太多一致性。不使用 musl、glibc、GNU coreutils 等的 Linux 发行版仍然是 Linux。

> Linux 移动操作系统还有很多，但据我所知，这些是主要的。这篇文章可能也有一些不准确的地方，我很久以前测试了其中一些，并且我从未真正运行过最后两个。

基于 AOSP 的移动操作系统是 Linux 发行版。

children.author: ipaddr
children.textRawHtml:
"Apps running in those compatibility layers are much less contained with less isolation from the Linux kernel, not more."

Being isolated a little bit more from the kernal offers an illusion of privacy meanwhile where you are, what you have installed, your photos and friends are available to other apps at a much higher level. I understand being able to slow down a nation state actor is important but most privacy concerns for average people happen at the OS level not the kernel.
children.textZhHtml:
“在这些兼容层中运行的应用程序受到的限制要少得多，与 Linux 内核的隔离也更少，而不是更多。”

与内核的隔离程度更高一点会带来一种隐私的错觉，同时您所在的位置、您安装的内容、您的照片和朋友都可以在更高级别上供其他应用程序使用。 我知道能够减慢国家行为者的速度很重要，但普通人的大多数隐私问题都发生在操作系统级别而不是内核。

children.author: HybridStatAnim8
children.textRawHtml: This claim is false. Isolation and protection from the kernel is vital and it is already targeted for exploitation, and will be targeted even more as time goes on. Properly updating the kernel and improving its isolation and security is the bare minimum for even below average users, not just for high threat models. The claims you are making are unsubstantiated.
children.textZhHtml: 这种说法是错误的。内核的隔离和保护至关重要，它已经成为被利用的目标，而且随着时间的推移，被针对的情况会越来越多。正确更新内核并改善其隔离和安全性，即使是对于低于平均水平的用户来说也是最低要求，而不仅仅是针对高威胁模型。你提出的这些说法毫无根据。

children.author: qurren
children.textRawHtml:
> These operating systems aren't compatible with most of the apps and services people want to use.

Exactly this.

If I can't run WeChat, Venmo, my brokerage app, WhatsApp, etc. it's a non-starter for me. I might as well not have a phone, because these apps are pretty much the only reason I carry around a phone.
children.textZhHtml:
> 这些操作系统与人们想要使用的大多数应用程序和服务不兼容。

正是如此。

如果我无法运行微信、Venmo、我的经纪应用程序、WhatsApp 等，那对我来说是不可能的。我还不如没有手机，因为这些应用程序几乎是我随身携带手机的唯一原因。

children.author: janvlug
children.textRawHtml:
I'm using a Librem 5 as my daily phone. PureOS is actively developed and based on Debian. Monthly development updates are published here: https://puri.sm/posts/tag/advanced-readers/

Personally, I do not use Android apps on the Librem 5, but Waydroid is available in the PureOS repository. Waydroid is a container-based approach to boot a full Android system on regular GNU/Linux systems running Wayland based desktop environments (like PureOS).

PureOS also provides convergence via Phosh. Convergence means here that the same app can be used on a phone and on a big screen, the GUI adjusts to the available screen size.

Phosh aims to provide a daily-usable, robust and easy to use graphical user environment for mobile devices running mainline Linux. Phosh was originally initiated by developers from Purism for the Librem 5 phone but is nowadays used on many different devices covering smartphones, tablets and convertibles. It has even been seen on laptops.
children.textZhHtml:
我使用 Librem 5 作为我的日常手机。 PureOS 正在积极开发并基于 Debian。每月开发更新发布在这里：https://puri.sm/posts/tag/advanced-readers/

就我个人而言，我不在 Librem 5 上使用 Android 应用程序，但 Waydroid 在 PureOS 存储库中可用。 Waydroid 是一种基于容器的方法，可在运行基于 Wayland 的桌面环境（如 PureOS）的常规 GNU/Linux 系统上启动完整的 Android 系统。

PureOS 还通过 Phosh 提供聚合。融合在这里意味着同一个应用程序可以在手机和大屏幕上使用，GUI 会根据可用的屏幕尺寸进行调整。

Phosh 旨在为运行主线 Linux 的移动设备提供日常可用、强大且易于使用的图形用户环境。 Phosh 最初是由 Purism 的开发人员为 Librem 5 手机发起的，但现在已用于许多不同的设备，包括智能手机、平板电脑和敞篷车。它甚至出现在笔记本电脑上。

children.author: grapheneos
children.textRawHtml:
> Waydroid is a container-based approach to boot a full Android system on regular GNU/Linux systems running Wayland based desktop environments (like PureOS).

No, it's only a partially working form of Android with the privacy/security model largely disabled and poor app compatibility. Waydroid is based on an ancient release of Android and disables the SELinux-based privacy/security model. It doesn't contain apps from each other and has far less protection for the Linux kernel from the apps. It has poor app compatibility and isn't a good approach to running Android in another OS. ChromeOS made a proper better Android container not losing the privacy/security model but migrated to using hardware accelerated virtual machines. It makes a lot more sense to use a VM since current era smartphone hardware fully supports it.

> PureOS also provides convergence via Phosh. Convergence means here that the same app can be used on a phone and on a big screen, the GUI adjusts to the available screen size.

Android Open Source Project has a desktop mode. It has a hardware-based virtualization layer for running desktop Linux applications too including GPU acceleration support.

> Phosh aims to provide a daily-usable, robust and easy to use graphical user environment for mobile devices running mainline Linux.

Android runs fine on mainline Linux. It doesn't require special kernels. That's tied to specific hardware rather than Android.

PureOS has far worse privacy and drastically worse security compared to iOS or AOSP. It's bringing the traditional atrocious privacy and security of desktops to mobile. Librem 5 also combines that with extraordinarily insecure hardware missing basic firmware updates and security protections. As a whole, these make it drastically easier to exploit devices. That includes going back to disk encryption which doesn't work for the average user due to them not using a strong passphrase and not protecting against data extraction with physical access unless the device is turned off.
children.textZhHtml:
> Waydroid 是一种基于容器的方法，可在运行基于 Wayland 的桌面环境（如 PureOS）的常规 GNU/Linux 系统上启动完整的 Android 系统。

不，它只是 Android 的部分工作形式，隐私/安全模型在很大程度上被禁用，应用程序兼容性也很差。 Waydroid 基于 Android 的一个古老版本，并禁用基于 SELinux 的隐私/安全模型。它不包含彼此的应用程序，并且对 Linux 内核免受应用程序的保护要少得多。它的应用程序兼容性很差，并且不是在其他操作系统中运行 Android 的好方法。 ChromeOS 制作了一个更好的 Android 容器，没有失去隐私/安全模型，而是迁移到使用硬件加速虚拟机。使用 VM 更有意义，因为当前时代的智能手机硬件完全支持它。

> PureOS 还通过 Phosh 提供聚合。融合在这里意味着同一个应用程序可以在手机和大屏幕上使用，GUI 会根据可用的屏幕尺寸进行调整。

Android 开源项目有桌面模式。它有一个基于硬件的虚拟化层，用于运行桌面 Linux 应用程序，包括 GPU 加速支持。

> Phosh 旨在为运行主线 Linux 的移动设备提供日常可用、强大且易于使用的图形用户环境。

Android 在主线 Linux 上运行良好。它不需要特殊的内核。这与特定硬件而不是 Android 相关。

与 iOS 或 AOSP 相比，PureOS 的隐私性和安全性要差得多。它将传统的台式机的残酷隐私和安全性带到了移动设备上。 Librem 5 还结合了极其不安全的硬件，缺少基本的固件更新和安全保护。总的来说，这些使得它大大更容易利用设备。这包括回到磁盘加密，这对普通用户不起作用，因为他们没有使用强密码，并且除非设备关闭，否则不会通过物理访问来防止数据提取。

children.author: baranul
children.textRawHtml: The way out is for people to support the various Linux phones. These Linux distros need to support and push Android compatibility, so that people can load F-Droid, Aurora, and Obtainium on them and get most of the Android apps they want. The ability to use both Linux and Android apps should satisfy nearly everyone. A strong message of consumer defiance needs to be sent.
children.textZhHtml: 出路在于人们支持各种Linux手机。这些Linux发行版需要支持并推动Android兼容性，以便人们可以在上面安装F-Droid、Aurora和Obtainium，并获取他们想要的大部分Android应用。能够同时使用Linux和Android应用应该能让几乎所有人都满意。必须发出一个强烈的消费者反抗信号。

children.author: HybridStatAnim8
children.textRawHtml:
The Android family of operating systems and the forks made from the android open source project are all linux distributions, and linux phones.

Using desktop linux phones and trying to force that as a norm would set privacy and security back substantially.

The inverse of what you suggest, which is Android with desktop linux app compatibility, would be a huge step forward, and is already much closer than you might think.

Modern phones have substantially better VM support in the hardware than in previous models, and it is maturing at a very fast rate. We would be able to run linux VMs on Android, paired with desktop mode, with evidence for USB passthrough for an externel GPU in AOSP

There is also evidence that we will be able to put desktop linux app icons on the Android homescreen and using them in an app-like fashion.

This would use the more secure host to run the VM for the less secure OS.
children.textZhHtml:
Android操作系统家族以及从Android开源项目衍生出的分支都是Linux发行版，也是Linux手机。

使用桌面Linux手机并试图将其强推为常态，将大幅倒退隐私和安全。

与你建议的相反，即Android兼容桌面Linux应用，将是一个巨大的进步，而且这已经比你想象的要近得多了。

现代手机在硬件上的虚拟机支持比以前的型号有了实质性的提升，并且正在以非常快的速度成熟。我们将能够在Android上运行Linux虚拟机，配合桌面模式，并且有证据表明AOSP中支持外部GPU的USB直通。

还有证据表明，我们将能够把桌面Linux应用图标放在Android主屏幕上，并像普通应用一样使用它们。

这将利用更安全的宿主机为安全性较低的操作系统运行虚拟机。

children.author: sambuccid
children.textRawHtml:
Personally I think that Android AOSP relies too much on the support from google, that has demonstrated to happily use anti-competitive practices and not be afraid to close and centralise AOSP as much as possible.

In a possible future where google decides to continue any new update of Android as closed source, will the community have enough people stepping in to support the development of AOSP? Will google allow the Play Store to run on AOSP? If not, do we have enough apps distributed outside of the Play Store to compensate?

My point is that for an OS that shares more code with desktop Linux (same apps, same DE, same terminal programs, ...) we can rely on the existing Linux community to maintain and advance the OS, and then adapt it for mobile, which I think takes considerably less resources
children.textZhHtml:
我个人认为 Android AOSP 过于依赖 Google 的支持，Google 已经表现出乐于使用反竞争做法，并且不惧怕尽可能关闭和中心化 AOSP。

在未来 Google 决定继续将 Android 的任何新更新作为闭源的情况下，社区是否会有足够的人介入来支持 AOSP 的开发？谷歌会允许 Play 商店在 AOSP 上运行吗？如果没有，我们是否有足够的应用程序在 Play 商店之外分发来弥补？

我的观点是，对于与桌面 Linux 共享更多代码的操作系统（相同的应用程序、相同的 DE、相同的终端程序......），我们可以依靠现有的 Linux 社区来维护和改进操作系统，然后将其适应移动设备，我认为这需要更少的资源

children.author: baranul
children.textRawHtml:
The problem is that the usage of Android should not be at the expense of users and developers rights to choose, freedom, or privacy. With that line crossed, Android becomes an unattractive option.

With an increase in mobile Linux (e.g. Mobian and others), they can and will get better. Customers and developers choice and freedom are being nullified, because there are few other options. That has to change.
children.textZhHtml:
问题在于，Android 的使用不应以牺牲用户和开发者的选择权、自由权或隐私权为代价。一旦越过这条线，Android 就会成为一个没有吸引力的选择。

随着移动 Linux（例如 Mobian 等）的增加，它们可以而且将会变得更好。客户和开发人员的选择和自由正在被取消，因为几乎没有其他选择。这必须改变。

children.author: HybridStatAnim8
children.textRawHtml: Android is not at the expense of either freedom or privacy. Desktop Linux OSs come at the cost of both. It would be better to direct effort to AOSP projects as it is a much better base to build from.
children.textZhHtml: Android 不会以牺牲自由或隐私为代价。桌面 Linux 操作系统是以两者为代价的。最好将精力集中在 AOSP 项目上，因为它是一个更好的构建基础。

children.author: hollow-moe
children.textRawHtml: And all are useless because you can't use your mandatory bank or gov id app.
children.textZhHtml: 所有这些都没有用，因为您无法使用强制性银行或政府 ID 应用程序。

children.author: karussell
children.textRawHtml:
Not useless. It is like the missing printer driver for Linux Desktop. It makes the experience ugly, but this is not the fault of the Linux OSes.

Also the bank should not require apps (instead they can offer hardware key support or desktop apps) and in fact some - at least in Germany - offer a different authentication possibility. Also the app for the German ID is published on fdroid and does not rely on Google services.
children.textZhHtml:
并非无用。这就像 Linux 桌面缺少打印机驱动程序一样。它使体验变得丑陋，但这不是 Linux 操作系统的错。

此外，银行不应该要求应用程序（相反，他们可以提供硬件密钥支持或桌面应用程序），事实上，一些应用程序（至少在德国）提供了不同的身份验证可能性。此外，德国 ID 的应用程序发布在 fdroid 上，不依赖 Google 服务。

children.author: trinix912
children.textRawHtml: Good for Germans then. Slovenian banks won't let you use physical 2FA authenticators (for personal accounts and maybe even business ones at this point) anymore and will also require you to constantly update their stupid app (I've had to replace some otherwise good phones because the OS version wasn't supported anymore).
children.textZhHtml: 那么对德国人来说有好处。斯洛文尼亚银行将不再允许您使用物理 2FA 身份验证器（目前适用于个人帐户，甚至可能是企业帐户），并且还会要求您不断更新他们愚蠢的应用程序（我不得不更换一些本来不错的手机，因为操作系统版本不再受支持）。

children.author: lbrito
children.textRawHtml:
Probably not the case for most people. I'm living abroad and had to do something on the Brazilian e-gov platform. To log in I had to confirm my ID with an Android app. Not only is it exclusively on Play store, but it also refuses to install on any rooted device, so I had to boot an old non-rooted Android I had stored somewhere.

I'm confident this is a very common experience worldwide, be it with gov IDs or banks.
children.textZhHtml:
对大多数人来说可能并非如此。我生活在国外，不得不在巴西电子政务平台上办理一些事务。为了登录，我必须使用一个Android应用来验证我的身份。它不仅仅在Play商店上架，而且拒绝在任何已root的设备上安装，所以我不得不启动一台存放在某处的旧的非root Android手机。

我确信这在全球范围内是非常普遍的经历，无论是政府身份验证还是银行。

children.author: int_19h
children.textRawHtml: The question of how useful or not it is is orthogonal to whether it is the "fault" of Linux. Users who can't use it because something they need just doesn't work won't change their minds because the blame lies elsewhere.
children.textZhHtml: 它有多大用处的问题与它是否是 Linux 的“错误”是正交的。因为他们需要的东西不起作用而无法使用它的用户不会改变主意，因为责任在其他地方。

children.author: mr_mitm
children.textRawHtml: There are plenty of banks in Germany which offer over-the-counter services, if you prefer to do banking as if it's 1999. Most of the time, when people say it's impossible to live without a smartphone, it's actually only impossible to enjoy the conveniences of the internet without a smartphone (at least in Germany). Besides these rentable scooters, I can't think of anything that actually requires a smartphone. Sure, you'll miss out on a lot of conveniences, but I remember a time where that was the norm, so it's not like it's unreasonable.
children.textZhHtml: 如果您喜欢像 1999 年一样处理银行业务，德国有很多银行提供柜台服务。大多数时候，当人们说没有智能手机就无法生活时，实际上没有智能手机就无法享受互联网的便利（至少在德国）。除了这些可出租的踏板车之外，我想不出还有什么真正需要智能手机的。当然，你会错过很多便利，但我记得那是常态，所以这并不是不合理。

children.author: arximboldi
children.textRawHtml:
I recently bought my first smartphone, just went for a refurbished Pixel 8 with GrapeheneOS.

To be honest, life without a smartphone was increasingly becoming a PITA.

For example, Ryanair doesn't accept printed tickets anymore.

A few clubs in Berlin (Tresor, Ohm, Oxi) have recently replaced their cloakroom by automated lockers that require a smartphone to operate.

I've encountered a few gyms (2 in Spain, 1 in USA) that use live-updated QR codes to enter the gym.

I did a project in the US and the client's office required a smartphone to open the door.

In Spain it's common since the pandemic to have restaurants that only offer the menu as QR code.

In fact, the pandemic was rough, as you had this system where you had to register with a QR code in most places. In many places they had a paper-registry that I could use, but often I would have to end up just using a friend's phone.

Plus all 4G dumbphones are crap compared to older 2G models. The few that exist are built really bad, designed for old people, lack features like T9. 2G is out already in great parts of the world.

To be honest, it saddens me deeply that the only way to live in society today involves carrying an internet-connected computer in your pocket. But it was just too much of burden... With GrapeheneOS the experience still feels somewhat acceptable and I get a somewhat similar feeling of control to what I get using NixOS on my laptop. But still...
children.textZhHtml:
我最近买了第一部智能手机，刚买了一台搭载 GrapeheneOS 的翻新 Pixel 8。

说实话，没有智能手机的生活越来越像 PITA。

例如，Ryanair 不再接受打印门票。

柏林的一些俱乐部（Tresor、Ohm、Oxi）最近用自动储物柜取代了衣帽间，需要智能手机才能进入

我遇到过一些健身房（西班牙 2 家，美国 1 家）使用实时更新的二维码进入健身房。

我在美国做了一个项目，客户的办公室需要智能手机才能开门。

在西班牙，自疫情以来，餐厅只提供二维码菜单的情况很常见。

事实上，疫情很严重，因为你必须使用这个系统在大多数地方都使用二维码注册。在很多地方，他们都有我可以使用的纸质注册表，但通常我只能使用朋友的手机。

此外，与旧的 2G 型号相比，所有 4G 哑电话都是垃圾。现有的少数产品质量非常差，专为老年人设计，缺乏像 T9 这样的功能。 2G 已经在世界大部分地区普及。

老实说，令我深感悲痛的是，当今社会生活的唯一方式就是在口袋里携带一台联网的计算机。但这实在是太大的负担......使用 GrapeheneOS 的体验仍然感觉可以接受，并且我得到了与在笔记本电脑上使用 NixOS 所获得的控制感有些相似的感觉。但仍然...

children.author: UnreachableCode
children.textRawHtml:
https://dumbermini.com/ (LineageOS fork)

https://commodore.net/callback/ (Sailfish OS)

I too am a GrapheneOS Pixel 8 user. However, I am starting to realise even a screen this large is a huge source of distraction and dopamine fracking that I don't have the time or desire for in my life. So I'm looking at these even dumber options. I just want my calendar and email, and maybe Signal. No web browser or socials.
children.textZhHtml:
https://dumbermini.com/（LineageOS 分支）

https://commodore.net/callback/（Sailfish OS）

我也是 GrapheneOS Pixel 8 用户。然而，我开始意识到，即使是这么大的屏幕也是一个巨大的干扰源和多巴胺压裂的来源，我在生活中没有时间或渴望。所以我正在考虑这些更愚蠢的选择。我只想要我的日历和电子邮件，也许还有 Signal。没有网络浏览器或社交功能。

stories.title: The labor share of income in the US is at its lowest post-war level
stories.titleZh: 美国劳动者收入占比处于战后最低水平

comments.author: legitster
comments.textRawHtml:
People are misreading the conclusion - the Covid related drop is normal and matches previous episodes, but the massive overall drop since 2000 is not.

The situation on the ground is unchanged - the amount of labor being generated per person has not really changed, but the overall pie has grown massively around us.
comments.textZhHtml:
人们误读了结论——与新冠疫情相关的下降是正常的，与以往的情况相符，但自2000年以来的整体大幅下降则不然。

实际情况并未改变——每个人产生的劳动量并没有真正改变，但我们周围的整个蛋糕却大幅增长了。

children.author: vannevar
children.textRawHtml:
This is consistent with the observation that the top 10% have captured a disproportionate share of GDP growth over the past few decades.

https://equitablegrowth.org/new-data-reveal-how-u-s-economic...

"The past three economic expansions have largely benefitted the top 10 percent. In each, the top decile received between 47 percent and 59 percent of all income growth in the expansion."
children.textZhHtml:
这与过去几十年来前10%的人群占据了不成比例的GDP增长份额的观察结果相一致。

https://equitablegrowth.org/new-data-reveal-how-u-s-economic...

“过去三次经济扩张主要惠及了前10%的人群。在每一次扩张中，收入最高的十分之一人群获得了所有收入增长的47%到59%。”

children.author: u1hcw9nx
children.textRawHtml:
Also note: Labor share has declined similarly across OECD countries for several decades.

Automation, robots, software etc. they are all capital share.
children.textZhHtml:
还需注意：几十年来，经合组织各国的劳动份额都出现了类似的下降。

自动化、机器人、软件等，它们都属于资本份额。

children.author: stymaar
children.textRawHtml:
> Automation, robots, software etc. they are all capital share.

I highly doubt automation and robots are a meaningful factor here, but IP and outsourcing have the exact same as automation.
children.textZhHtml:
> 自动化、机器人、软件等，它们都属于资本份额。

我非常怀疑自动化和机器人在这里是否是一个有意义的因素，但知识产权和外包产生的影响与自动化完全相同。

children.author: boelboel
children.textRawHtml: New factories use very few people, part of the reason why it's difficult for many countries to industrialize like South Korea or China did (climbing manufacturing ladder).
children.textZhHtml: 新工厂使用的工人非常少，这也是许多国家难以像韩国或中国那样实现工业化（攀登制造业阶梯）的部分原因。

children.author: stymaar
children.textRawHtml:
> New factories use very few people

That's both true and false. Yes they need very few people to operate, but building and maintaining still need a lot of people.
children.textZhHtml:
> 新工厂使用的人员很少

这既是正确的也是错误的。是的，他们需要很少的人来操作，但是建设和维护仍然需要很多人。

children.author: jjk166
children.textRawHtml: They need fewer people to build and to maintain than older ones did. Further the jobs from building the factory are temporary.
children.textZhHtml: 与老工厂相比，它们需要更少的工人来建设和维护。此外，建厂带来的就业岗位是临时性的。

children.author: stymaar
children.textRawHtml:
> Further the jobs from building the factory are temporary.

This is correct, and it has an impact on local employment and social dynamics, but not at the country level.

> They need fewer people to build and to maintain than older ones did.

That's absolutely not true. Quite the opposite. You do need less people to build and maintain a modern plant than to operate an factory in the past.

Also, you need to clarify what you mean by “older”, because heavy industries have automated steadily between the 50s and the 80s, and that process was mostly achieved by the 90s.

And I can't think of an industry that was still labor intensive by the 20s and that has been more impacted by automation than offshoring.
children.textZhHtml:
>此外，建厂带来的就业岗位是临时性的。

这没错，它对当地就业和社会动态有影响，但在国家层面则不然。

>与老工厂相比，它们需要更少的工人来建设和维护。

这绝对不对。恰恰相反。建设和维护一座现代化工厂确实比过去运营一家工厂需要的人少。

另外，你需要澄清一下“老”是什么意思，因为重工业在50年代到80年代期间一直在稳步实现自动化，而且这个过程在90年代基本就完成了。

而且，我想不出有哪个行业到了20年代仍然是劳动密集型的，并且受自动化的影响比离岸外包更大。

children.author: SideQuark
children.textRawHtml:
There’s plenty of papers showing exactly this. What do you think has driven productivity? People simply bring smarter?

The fact is capital expenditure from company or investors has bought machinery, compute, pipelines, transport, and massive investment to make those workers more productive for decades. As such, the returns to capital as a share has increased. Those places able to deploy capital to add productivity win over those that don’t.

And real total remuneration across all quintiles has increased significantly. BLS among others has all historical data to check.

If/when there’s a period where there isn’t more gains to be had by more investment per worker, and workers become more productive via their own skill (education, diet, genetic implants,…), then more returns will flow that direction.

This is all well known, and easily checked.
children.textZhHtml:
有很多论文准确地说明了这一点。您认为是什么推动了生产力的提高？人们只是变得更聪明？

事实是，公司或投资者的资本支出购买了机械、计算、管道、运输和大规模投资，以提高这些工人几十年来的生产力。因此，每股资本回报率有所增加。那些能够部署资本来提高生产力的地方胜过那些没有能力部署资本的地方。

所有五分位数的实际总薪酬都显着增加。 BLS 等有所有历史数据可供检查。

如果/当有一个时期，每个工人的更多投资并不能带来更多收益，并且工人通过自己的技能（教育、饮食、基因植入……）提高生产力，那么更多的回报将会流向这个方向。

这都是众所周知的，并且很容易检查。

children.author: stymaar
children.textRawHtml:
> What do you think has driven productivity?

“Productivity” is a terrible metric for such a discussion, it's not what it sounds, it's merely “real” GDP/worked hours.

> The fact is capital expenditure from company or investors has bought machinery, compute, pipelines, transport, and massive investment to make those workers more productive for decades. As such, the returns to capital as a share has increased. Those places able to deploy capital to add productivity win over those that don’t.

This is the fairy tale you learn in econ 101, but in reality that's not what happened in the period, the businesses that won during the 2000-24 period (the so-called “tech” companies) were companies for which capex were well below average.

> This is all well known, and easily checked.

That's not “well known”, you're just saying the gospel. It doesn't matter if it's contradicted by evidences, you believe that how the world work so it must be true.
children.textZhHtml:
> 您认为是什么推动了生产力？

对于这样的讨论来说，“生产力”是一个糟糕的指标，它并不像听起来那样，它只是“真实的”GDP/工作时间。

>事实是，公司或投资者的资本支出购买了机械、计算、管道、运输和大规模投资，以提高这些工人几十年来的生产力。因此，每股资本回报率有所增加。那些能够部署资本来提高生产力的地方战胜了那些没有能力部署资本的地方。

这是你在经济101中学到的童话故事，但实际上这并不是那个时期发生的事情，在2000-24年期间获胜的企业（所谓的“科技”公司）都是资本支出远低于平均水平的公司。

>这都是众所周知的，并且很容易检查。

这不是“很好”众所周知”，你只是在说福音。即使与证据相矛盾也没关系，你相信世界是如何运转的，所以它一定是真的。

children.author: mech998877
children.textRawHtml:
Robots (and other tools) are capital. A way I think of it:

if you formed a co-op of sorts, with let's say 20 people, starting with no land ownership and hardly any tools, they could try to make a business. Whatever they end up starting would be a fairly low-productivity business- washing windows, janitorial services, lawn service, etc. The more tools and land a co-op has to work with, the more productive they can be. With a few million dollars up-front they could have built a factory instead.

The increased productivity generated can be attributed to the capital share of income.
children.textZhHtml:
机器人（和其他工具）是资本。我的想法是：

如果你组建了一个合作社，假设有 20 个人，一开始没有土地所有权，几乎没有任何工具，他们可以尝试做生意。无论他们最终开始做什么，都将是一项生产力相当低的业务——清洗窗户、清洁服务、草坪服务等。合作社需要使用的工具和土地越多，他们的生产力就越高。只要预付几百万美元，他们就可以建造一座工厂。

生产率的提高可归因于收入的资本份额。

children.author: stymaar
children.textRawHtml:
That's econ 101. But that doesn't mean that's what happened in the real world.

The thing is you won't be able to name a single industry for which automation has had a significant impact on labor productivity during the 2000-2020.

The US lost almost half of its manufacturing jobs in the 2000s and it has pretty much nothing to do with productivity, and everything to do with businesses moving their production abroad.

Robots can have a huge impact in the coming decades, but you can't argue that they had negligible impact on the past two decades.
children.textZhHtml:
这是经济 101。但这并不意味着这就是发生在

问题是，在 2000 年至 2020 年期间，你无法说出自动化对劳动生产率产生重大影响的哪个行业。

美国在 2000 年代失去了近一半的制造业工作岗位，这与生产力几乎无关，而与企业将生产转移到国外有关。

机器人可以在未来几十年产生巨大影响，但你不能说它们对过去二十年的影响微乎其微。

children.author: 9x39
children.textRawHtml:
Automation isn't foreign to the topic. There's some discussion here that refrains from estimating too hard, but I think it's closer to outsourcing's effect:

https://www.stlouisfed.org/on-the-economy/2024/jun/worker-sc...

Outsourcing and automation both reduce worker leverage, which reduces wages, which could explain reduced labor share. I'm not sure how one would weight it all.
children.textZhHtml:
自动化对于这个主题来说并不陌生。这里有一些讨论，但我认为它更接近外包的效果：

https://www.stlouisfed.org/on-the-economy/2024/jun/worker-sc...

外包和自动化都会降低工人的杠杆率，从而降低工资，这可以解释劳动力份额的减少。我不确定如何权衡这一切。

children.author: stretchwithme
children.textRawHtml:
Employee compensation comes from capital. And employees are working at companies that provide robots, etc.

There's a return on capital than is not spent on employees. That reflects how much capital is growing and how much can be spent on employees in the future.
children.textZhHtml:
员工薪酬来自资本。而员工正是在提供机器人等设备的公司工作。

有一部分资本回报没有花在员工身上。这反映了资本增长了多少，以及未来能有多少花在员工身上。

children.author: vannevar
children.textRawHtml:
>And employees are working at companies that provide robots, etc.

Just as are the top executives. And the shareholders that have put money into companies that provide "robots, etc.". All these people, including labor, are stakeholders. If there was 5% GDP growth that got reflected as 5% growth in net earnings for the company, one would expect that all the stakeholders would see roughly a 5% increase in their personal earnings from the company. The dollar amount would be higher for higher earners (5% of $1M is greater than 5% of $50k), but the percentage increase would be roughly in line. The real world results are not even close to this "rising tide lifts all boats" ideal.
children.textZhHtml:
>员工在提供机器人等的公司工作。

就像高层管理人员一样。还有那些向提供“机器人等”的公司投入资金的股东。所有这些人，包括劳工，都是利益相关者。如果 GDP 增长 5%，反映为公司净利润增长 5%，那么人们会期望所有利益相关者从公司获得的个人收益将增加大约 5%。对于高收入者来说，金额会更高（100 万美元的 5% 大于 5 万美元的 5%），但百分比增长大致一致。现实世界的结果甚至与“水涨船高”的理想状态相差甚远。

children.author: 9x39
children.textRawHtml:
They didn't make the rising tide analogy, I read it as how much could be captured by labor if we increased leverage.

In any case, it doesn't follow that wages grow with earnings. Wages have historically been a lagging indicator.
children.textZhHtml:
他们并没有用“水涨船高”的比喻，我把它理解为如果我们增加杠杆，劳动力能获取多少收益。

无论如何，工资随收益增长并不是必然的。从历史上看，工资一直是一个滞后指标。

children.author: vannevar
children.textRawHtml:
>They didn't make the rising tide analogy, I read it as how much could be captured by labor if we increased leverage.

Fair point, though it's not completely clear from the comment.

>Wages have historically been a lagging indicator.

Of course, companies don't know in advance that they're going to have GDP-assisted growth. My point was that growth on the back of GDP growth is a collective windfall, and you'd expect it to be evenly distributed. But it clearly isn't.
children.textZhHtml:
>他们并没有用“水涨船高”的比喻，我把它理解为如果我们增加杠杆，劳动力能获取多少收益。

说得有道理，不过从评论里看不太出来。

>从历史上看，工资一直是一个滞后指标。

当然，公司事先并不知道它们会获得GDP的助力增长。我的观点是，依托GDP增长带来的增长是一种集体红利，按理说应该被平均分配。但事实显然并非如此。

children.author: PaulDavisThe1st
children.textRawHtml:
> Employee compensation comes from capital.

All human collective endeavors (with few exceptions) require 3 kinds of human-related input: capital, labor and ideas.

Nobody puts their capital into an endeavor in which the plan is for the that capital to provide renumeration for the labor for more than the shortest possible time (*). The goal is always to generate revenue in sufficient volume to pay for the labor, and when that goal is met, that success is a function of all 3 kinds of contribution.

So no, employee compensation does not come from capital, but from revenue that results from the successful interaction of capital, labor and ideas.

(*) non-profits would be an obvious exception, except that nobody actually talks about investing capital in such organizations, we just make "donations" or "grants". That money plays the same role as capital, however.
children.textZhHtml:
> 员工薪酬来自资本。

所有人类集体事业（除了少数例外）都需要 3 种与人类相关的投入：资本、劳动力和创意。

没有人将其资本投入到一项计划中，计划让资本在最短的时间内为劳动力提供报酬 (*)。我们的目标始终是产生足够的收入来支付劳动力，而当这个目标实现时，成功是所有三种贡献的函数。

所以，员工的报酬不是来自资本，而是来自资本、劳动力和想法成功相互作用所产生的收入。

(*)非营利组织将是一个明显的例外，除了没有人真正谈论在这些组织中投资资本，我们只是进行“捐赠”或“赠款”。然而，这些钱与资本起着同样的作用。

children.author: wolvesechoes
children.textRawHtml:
> Automation, robots, software etc.

Nah, it is just capitalism at work. Winner takes all.
children.textZhHtml:
> 自动化、机器人、软件等。

不，这只是资本主义在发挥作用。赢家通吃。

children.author: cherryteastain
children.textRawHtml:
"Top 10%" is such a misleading slice here. The guy who's at the 9.99th percentile is a normal salaried worker not doing better. The gains are entirely concentrated in the tiny billionaire slice buried inside that 10%. In fact wage growth for the top decile has been recently slower than bottom deciles [1]. Incomes still grow fast in the top decile, but mostly due to assets. And those assets are disproportionately in the hands of the billionaire slice of that top decile.

[1] https://www.epi.org/publication/strong-wage-growth-for-low-w...
children.textZhHtml:
在这里，“前10%”是一个极具误导性的划分。处于第9.99百分位的人只是一个普通的工薪阶层，日子并没有过得更好。收益完全集中在那10%中隐藏的极小部分的亿万富翁群体身上。事实上，最近最高十分位人群的工资增长速度甚至低于最低十分位人群[1]。最高十分位人群的收入仍在快速增长，但主要是由于资产。而这些资产不成比例地掌握在那最高十分位中的亿万富翁群体手中。

[1] https://www.epi.org/publication/strong-wage-growth-for-low-w...

stories.title: For first time, a cell built from scratch grows and divides
stories.titleZh: 第一次，从头开始构建的细胞生长并分裂

comments.author: JumpCrisscross
comments.textRawHtml:
“This was where the field had been stuck for some time. Researchers before Adamala had figured out different ways to feed and grow synthetic cells and to replicate their DNA. But cell division is a different beast. A typical cell reorganizes its cytoskeleton — a network of protein fibers that provide structural support — to halve its DNA and split. Synthetic biologists could not figure out how to get their cells to undergo this complex process.

So Adamala decided to ditch the cytoskeleton. One day, while tearing through the literature, she came across an interesting mechanism in a paper (opens a new tab). By attaching protein tags to a cell membrane, the synthetic biologist Reinhard Lipowsky (opens a new tab) at the Max Planck Institute of Colloids and Interfaces attracted other proteins to crowd around and physically bend the membrane, forcing the cell to divide. Following this approach, Adamala tweaked a cell-membrane protein and tested it in her protocells. After several tries, it worked.“

This is the novel bit.
comments.textZhHtml:
“这正是该领域停滞不前的一段时间。在Adamala之前，研究人员已经找到了喂养和培育合成细胞以及复制其DNA的不同方法。但细胞分裂完全是另一回事。典型的细胞会重组其细胞骨架——一种提供结构支持的蛋白纤维网络——以使DNA减半并分裂。合成生物学家一直无法弄清楚如何让他们的细胞经历这一复杂过程。

于是Adamala决定放弃细胞骨架。有一天，她在大量阅读文献时，偶然发现了一篇论文中一个有趣的机制（打开新标签页）。马克斯·普朗克胶体与界面研究所的合成生物学家Reinhard Lipowsky（打开新标签页）通过在细胞膜上附着蛋白质标签，吸引其他蛋白质聚集在周围并物理性地弯曲细胞膜，从而迫使细胞分裂。遵循这一方法，Adamala对一种细胞膜蛋白进行了微调，并在她的原始细胞中进行了测试。经过几次尝试，终于成功了。”

这就是新颖之处。

children.author: dnautics
children.textRawHtml: without criticising the work (its very cool and a very important first step) they haven't figured out division yet, which is kind of important.
children.textZhHtml: 在不批评这项工作的情况下（它非常酷，也是非常重要的第一步），他们还没有弄清楚细胞分裂，而这其实挺重要的。

children.author: IAmBroom
children.textRawHtml: Lots of missing steps before it is "created life". Which the researchers admit in the very start.
children.textZhHtml: 距离“创造生命”还有很多缺失的步骤。研究人员在文章开头就承认了这一点。

children.author: SwtCyber
children.textRawHtml: The interesting bit is that they didn't solve division by rebuilding the whole cytoskeleton, they sort of sidestepped it
children.textZhHtml: 有意思的是，他们并没有通过重建整个细胞骨架来解决分裂问题，而是某种程度上绕开了它

children.author: ezst
children.textRawHtml: (opens a new tab)
children.textZhHtml: （打开新标签页）

children.author: khriss
children.textRawHtml: Yeah, I was wondering about that as well. Some weird AI transcriber?
children.textZhHtml: 是啊，我也在想这个问题。是什么奇怪的AI转录工具吗？

children.author: jmaw
children.textRawHtml: I interpreted it as the author adding some internal dialog about how they want to do more research on the article/person in question so they were opening up a new tab so they could learn more. But I can see how this could certainly be some copy/paste artifact.
children.textZhHtml: 我把它理解为作者添加了一些内心独白，说他们想对相关文章或人物做进一步研究，所以打开了一个新标签页以便了解更多。但我也能看出这确实可能是某种复制粘贴留下的痕迹。

children.author: JumpCrisscross

It’s this. I noticed after posting, found it funny and so left it in.
children.textZhHtml:

就是这个。我发完之后才注意到，觉得挺有意思的，就留着了。

children.author: kridsdale1
children.textRawHtml: More likely stuff that gets picked up when you copy and paste. I’ve seen that happen in the Google Chat electron app.
children.textZhHtml: 更可能是复制粘贴时带入的多余内容。我在Google Chat的electron应用里见过这种情况。

children.author: satvikpendem
children.textRawHtml: Maybe an accessibility feature for TTS or blind users?
children.textZhHtml: 也许是针对TTS或盲人用户的无障碍功能？

children.author: sourpanda
children.textRawHtml: that's exactly it, not AI at all. If you inspect any link in the article it shows it as screen-reader-text
children.textZhHtml: 确实如此，完全不是AI。如果你检查文章里的任何链接，会发现它显示为screen-reader-text

comments.author: merksittich
comments.textRawHtml:
Science News has a more balanced take, with additional quotes from peers.

> Some have also grumbled about Adamala’s efforts to draw attention to the work, which she says was rejected by Cell after one reviewer said SpudCells were not real biology. She then sent the 190-page manuscript to journalists, under embargo, even before she had uploaded it to the preprint server bioRxiv, where her colleagues could read and assess it. She says her group will submit it to a new journal soon. “It’s an unusual way of doing things,” says Kerstin Göpfrich, a synthetic biologist at Heidelberg University.

https://www.science.org/content/article/lab-created-spudcell...
comments.textZhHtml:
《科学新闻》的报道更为客观平衡，并补充了同行的更多引言。

> 还有一些人对Adamala为引起对该研究的关注所做的努力颇有微词。她表示，该研究曾被《细胞》杂志拒稿，因为一位审稿人认为SpudCells不是真正的生物学。随后，她将这份长达190页的手稿在保密状态下发送给记者，甚至在她将其上传到预印本服务器bioRxiv供同行阅读和评估之前。她说她的团队很快会将其提交给一家新期刊。“这是一种不寻常的做法，”海德堡大学的合成生物学家Kerstin Göpfrich说。

https://www.science.org/content/article/lab-created-spudcell...

children.author: cmiles74
children.textRawHtml:
I thought the NY Times article was pretty good, they had some nice illustrations.

https://www.nytimes.com/interactive/2026/07/01/science/spudc...
children.textZhHtml:
我觉得《纽约时报》的文章写得相当不错，里面有一些很棒的插图。

https://www.nytimes.com/interactive/2026/07/01/science/spudc...

children.author: abrookewood
children.textRawHtml: That is way more comprehensible for me at least.
children.textZhHtml: 至少对我来说，这样要好理解得多。

children.author: oliverx0
children.textRawHtml: Crazy that a Cell reviewer would claim synthetic biology is not biology
children.textZhHtml: 《细胞》的审稿人竟然声称合成生物学不是生物学，简直不可思议

children.author: cperciva
children.textRawHtml:
My paper demonstrating a side channel attack on RSA via hyperthreading was rejected from the crypto preprint archive on the basis that it was "not cryptography".

(Reviewers at J.Crypto subsequently sat on it for a year and then suggested I submit it to a journal on CPU microarchitecture instead.)

Novel research is uniquely susceptible to "cool but it's not part of our field", because that critique is entirely correct until the research gets published!
children.textZhHtml:
我写了一篇论文，演示了通过超线程对RSA进行侧信道攻击，结果被密码学预印本档案库拒稿，理由是“不属于密码学”。

（《密码学杂志》的审稿人随后把这篇论文搁置了一年，然后建议我改投一本关于CPU微架构的期刊。）

新颖的研究特别容易遭到“很酷，但这不属于我们的领域”这种评价，因为在研究成果发表之前，这种批评完全正确！

children.author: _zoltan_
children.textRawHtml: our paper to a database venue about bringing GPU support to Presto was rejected. one of the reviewers wrote, and I quote verbatim: "the topic of the paper is too practical". I just couldn't help but laughed at it.
children.textZhHtml: 我们向一个数据库会议提交了一篇关于为Presto引入GPU支持的论文，结果被拒了。其中一位审稿人写道，我原话引用：“论文的主题太实用了”。我简直忍不住笑了。

children.author: hoppp
children.textRawHtml:
Too practical haha
Maybe they just wanted hype?
children.textZhHtml: 太实用了哈哈 也许他们只是想炒作一下？

children.author: coldtea
children.textRawHtml: Or perhaps they wanted something theoritical that advances the state of the art in database design and architecture (which is the whole purpose of academic reasearch/conference/etc) - not merely some report describing doing something that's already been done for a different DB.
children.textZhHtml: 又或者他们想要的是某种能推动数据库设计与架构前沿发展的理论成果（这正是学术研究/会议等的全部目的）——而不仅仅是一份报告，描述在另一个数据库上做了些别人已经做过的事情。

children.author: _zoltan_
children.textRawHtml: that's a very condescending view from you, to be honest.
children.textZhHtml: 说实话，你这看法真是太居高临下了。

stories.title: .self: A new top-level domain designed to support self-hosting
stories.titleZh: .self：旨在支持自托管的新顶级域名

comments.author: goldenarm
comments.textRawHtml:
Remember when the .tk TLD became free 20 years ago ? Every hobbyist took one, then scammers followed, then Facebook and antiviruses started blocking it.

I remember publishing a website for a class on my .tk domain, the teacher couldn't open it and I almost got a failing grade because of it.
comments.textZhHtml:
还记得20年前.tk顶级域名免费开放的时候吗？每个爱好者都注册了一个，接着骗子们蜂拥而至，然后Facebook和杀毒软件就开始屏蔽它。

我记得当时用我的.tk域名发布了一个课堂网站，结果老师打不开，我差点因此不及格。

children.author: mort96
children.textRawHtml:
A friend almost failed an IT class because his website didn't render at all in IE6. This was during the time of IE9. The teacher just hadn't updated their browser in a long time.

I don't get how you get to be an IT teacher without knowing the most basic troubleshooting steps to get assignments to run.
children.textZhHtml:
我一个朋友差点没通过信息技术课，因为他的网站在IE6上完全无法渲染。那时候已经是IE9的时代了。纯粹是老师太久没更新浏览器了。

我真搞不懂，一个连让作业运行起来的最基础的故障排除步骤都不懂的人，是怎么当上信息技术老师的。

children.author: hilariously
children.textRawHtml:
I left community college after a week because my "computer" teacher required us to change our monitors to 640x480 and print out ever step that we completed in things like Notepad or Configuring the Desktop and then every day we'd punch it out and would add it to a three ring binder of all the things we've done.

Full Color.
children.textZhHtml:
我在社区大学只待了一个星期就退学了，因为我的“计算机”老师要求我们把显示器分辨率调到640x480，并且把我们在记事本或桌面配置等任务中完成的每一步都打印出来，然后每天打孔装订，加到一本记录我们所有操作的三孔活页夹里。

全彩打印。

children.author: yoz-y
children.textRawHtml: 640x480 in 16 colors is the resolution as it was prescribed in the Bible.
children.textZhHtml: 16色下的640x480分辨率，简直就是圣经里规定的标准。

children.author: markhahn
children.textRawHtml: though shalt not wear garments of mixed pallettes
children.textZhHtml: 汝不可穿混色之衣

children.author: jagged-chisel
children.textRawHtml: Amen
children.textZhHtml: 阿门

children.author: tuwtuwtuwtuw
children.textRawHtml:
Terry Davis quote?

> God said 640×480 16 colour is a covenant like circumcision.

https://clotmag.com/oped/deus-ex-machina-deconstructing-digi...
children.textZhHtml:
Terry Davis的语录？
