children.author: wccrawford
children.textRawHtml: I took a computer class in college (like 25 years ago now?) and corrected the teacher multiple times every class. And it was like 95% things that were in the book that was issued, so I could even point out the page it was on. It was absolutely embarrassing.
children.textZhHtml: 我大学时上过一门计算机课（大概25年前了吧？），每节课都要纠正老师好几次。而且95%的内容都是发的教材里写过的，我甚至能指出具体在第几页。简直太让人尴尬了。

children.author: Tor3
children.textRawHtml:
In college I one year had a physics teacher who refused to believe that you could hear the harmonics of a guitar string simply by lightly touching the string at the right place, e.g. in the middle (12th fret) to hear the octave.  Nothing could convince him that this was possible.  
That year was when several of us spent a good part of the week bowling in a nearby bowling hall, either because we didn't have to take the class (the curriculum was years behind what we had already done before college), or the teachers were so incompetent that there was no point attending.
Fortunately the other educational years (everything after middle school, that year excepted) were great though.
children.textZhHtml:
上大学那年，我遇到个物理老师，他死活不信只要轻轻按住吉他弦的正确位置，比如中间（第12品），就能听到泛音，也就是八度音。什么都无法说服他相信这是可能的。  
那年，我们好几个人一星期有大半天都在附近的保龄球馆打保龄球，要么是因为不用上课（课程内容比我们上大学前学的落后好几年），要么是因为老师太无能，上课根本没意义。
不过幸运的是，其他学年（初中毕业后的所有年份，除了那年）都挺棒的。

children.author: chasd00
children.textRawHtml: if i may rant, my middle school and high school aged kids have to literally take pictures of their homework assignments with their chromebook camera then put it the pictures in a google slides deck then submit the deck through a form all to just turn in their paper HW assignment! ridiculous.
children.textZhHtml: 容我吐槽一下，我上初中和高中的孩子们居然得用Chromebook的摄像头把纸质作业拍下来，然后把照片插到Google Slides演示文稿里，再通过表单提交这个演示文稿，就为了交个纸质作业！太荒谬了。

children.author: Aeolun
children.textRawHtml: Can’t you just hand it in?
children.textZhHtml: 你就不能直接交上去吗？

children.author: mathstuf
children.textRawHtml: Heh…I once was in a state-level coding event (it was a small portion of a larger competition) where half of the test was turning in code on a CD during the competition, with the written half during the event. My CD was deemed unusable for whatever reason (it had worked on XP and Fedora 6 or 7 at home) and didn't count towards my score. I still got second in the event. I declined to continue because I couldn't trust that the judges would be able judge my submission fairly and that with half of my score missing I still got second that I didn't need to prove anything else at the cost of more after-school practice hours and wrecking my perfect attendance record during my senior year to travel to nationals.
children.textZhHtml: 呵……我曾经参加过一次州级的编程比赛（那只是一个大型比赛的一小部分），其中一半的考核是在比赛期间把代码刻录到光盘上交，另一半是笔试。不知什么原因，我的光盘被判定为无法使用（在家里XP和Fedora 6或7系统上明明能正常运行），因此没计入我的成绩。但我还是拿了第二名。我拒绝继续参赛了，因为我不相信评委能公平评判我的作品，而且既然缺了一半的成绩我还能拿第二，我也没必要再牺牲更多课后练习时间，甚至打破我高三的完美出勤记录去全国赛证明什么了。

children.author: csense
children.textRawHtml: Perfect attendance is not a good goal to aspire to.  Kids force themselves (or get forced by parents) to go to school while sick, which is probably bad for their health and also risks everybody else's health.
children.textZhHtml: 全勤并不是一个值得追求的好目标。孩子们带病上学，要么是强迫自己，要么是被父母逼着去，这不仅可能损害他们自己的健康，还会危及他人的健康。

children.author: jon-wood
children.textRawHtml: I dropped out of college (the UK version, I guess equivalent to senior high school in the US) shortly after discovering that the final assessment of my Computing project would be performed by the examiner reading a printed version of the source code, without ever executing it, because the exam board were so scared of examiners computers being destroyed.
children.textZhHtml: 我大学辍学了（英国的大学体系，大概相当于美国的高中），起因是我发现我的计算项目最终评估，竟然是让考官阅读打印出来的源代码，而且根本不运行，因为考试委员会太害怕考官的电脑被搞坏了。

children.author: alfiedotwtf
children.textRawHtml: You haven’t lived life until you’ve sat a Data Structures and Algorithms exam having to write all coding solutions with paper and pen
children.textZhHtml: 没经历过数据结构与算法考试、只能拿纸笔手写所有代码解答，就不算真正活过。

children.author: mghackerlady
children.textRawHtml: When was this? If this was before virtualisation was common I can maybe understand that but any time in the last 20 years is pretty dumb and the last 10 so braindead I question if they would've been able to judge things properly
children.textZhHtml: 这是什么时候的事？如果这是在虚拟化普及之前，我或许还能理解，但过去20年里的任何时候这么做都挺蠢的，而过去10年更是愚蠢至极，我甚至怀疑他们是否还能正确判断事物。

children.author: jon-wood
children.textRawHtml: Oh this was in 2000, when virtualisation was only just becoming accessible so I can get of get the justification. It still made the entire exercise in writing some software feel pointless when I knew it would never get executed by anyone but myself.
children.textZhHtml: 哦，那是在2000年，当时虚拟化才刚刚变得可用，所以我能理解其合理性。但这依然让我觉得编写这些软件的整个过程毫无意义，因为我知道除了我自己，永远不会有人去执行它。

children.author: L_226
children.textRawHtml:
Reminds me of Lord Vetinari from Discworld, reading sheet music instead of listening to adulterated performances by fat sweaty men squeezing the music through some tubes.

Executing the code in your head removed from the nuances of hardware, CPU architecture and compiler versions seems like a virtuous pursuit (?)
children.textZhHtml:
这让我想起了《碟形世界》里的维蒂纳里领主，他宁愿看乐谱，也不愿听那些肥胖多汗的男人通过管子挤出来的变味演奏。

在脑海中执行代码，脱离硬件的细微差别、CPU架构和编译器版本，似乎是一种高尚的追求（？）

children.author: zbentley
children.textRawHtml:
> Executing the code in your head removed from the nuances of hardware, CPU architecture and compiler versions seems like a virtuous pursuit

…and that’s how we got Java :p

And stuff like Pascal, too, so it’s not all bad.
children.textZhHtml:
> 在脑海中执行代码，脱离硬件的细微差别、CPU架构和编译器版本，似乎是一种高尚的追求

……所以我们才搞出了Java :p

还有Pascal之类的，所以也不全是坏事。

children.author: rogerrogerr
children.textRawHtml: Does high school attendance matter for anything? Genuine question. Always seemed like pre-college schooling always wanted you to think everything was more important long-term than it really was.
children.textZhHtml: 高中出勤率到底有什么意义吗？真心发问。总觉得高中教育总是想让你觉得一切从长远来看都比实际情况重要得多。

stories.title: European digital ID wallets rely on safety services of Google and Apple
stories.titleZh: 欧洲数字身份钱包依赖谷歌和苹果的安全服务

comments.author: Luker88
comments.textRawHtml:
The EU reference for wallets strictly required google play services https://github.com/eu-digital-identity-wallet/eudi-app-andro...

So Italy's IO app https://github.com/pagopa/io-app (wallet, documents, age verification) continuously refuses the users' request for GrapheneOS support and requires google.

Nothing will change until the lawsuits start coming in.

The only hope is the motorola/grapheneOS collaboration and consumer associations, that might sue for anticompetitive behavior.

Make noise on any channel for the apps that require play services, it will help in the future if the lawsuits start, since it will show user support for the initiative.
comments.textZhHtml:
欧盟严格要求的钱包参考 Google Play 服务 https://github.com/eu-digital-identity-wallet/eudi-app-andro...

因此，意大利的 IO 应用https://github.com/pagopa/io-app（钱包、文档、年龄验证）不断拒绝用户对 GrapheneOS 支持的请求，并需要 Google。

在诉讼开始之前，一切都不会改变。

唯一的希望是motorola/grapheneOS 合作和消费者协会，可能会起诉反竞争行为。

在需要播放服务的应用程序的任何渠道上发出噪音，如果诉讼开始，这将在未来有所帮助，因为这将表明用户对该计划的支持。

children.author: WhyNotHugo
children.textRawHtml:
The issue isn't just the technical dependency.

It's also the fact that it forces each citizen to pay a few hundred Euros to companies which then campaign against their very rights.

Citizens get no support of any kind in case of issues, and has to enter a contractual agreement which is ridiculously asymmetrical, where the company has little to no responsibility of any kind, but has very ample rights to track the other party in extremely creepy ways.
children.textZhHtml:
问题不仅仅是技术依赖性。

事实上，它迫使每个公民向公司支付几百欧元，而这些公司随后会侵犯他们的权利。

公民在出现问题时得不到任何形式的支持，并且必须签订一份极其不对称的合同协议，该公司几乎没有任何责任，但有足够的权利以极其令人毛骨悚然的方式追踪对方方法。

children.author: chadgpt3
children.textRawHtml: It doesn't force them to, strictly speaking - they also have the choice to sue the government
children.textZhHtml: 严格来说，这并没有强迫他们 - 他们也可以选择起诉政府

children.author: spwa4
children.textRawHtml:
But ... the alternative is that the government actually pays a bit of money to fix the situation! To support their solutions. To actually develop them for enough devices. To secure them ... Plus the services the government made are way more invasive than the Google/Apple ones.

In addition to the money, actually using them would be hundreds of times more complex, and they don't have the provisions Google has, for example accessibility and security services (like actually stopping people stealing accounts on a large scale). All of this can be done, easily even, but it isn't. Politicians don't want to.

https://www.itsme-id.com/business/platform/identification

https://france-identite.gouv.fr/

https://english.rekenkamer.nl/latest/news/2023/03/29/digital...
children.textZhHtml:
但是......另一种选择是政府实际上支付一点钱来解决这个问题！支持他们的解决方案。实际开发它们以适应足够的设备。为了保护它们......加上政府提供的服务比谷歌/苹果的服务更具侵入性。

除了金钱之外，实际使用它们会复杂数百倍，而且它们没有谷歌的规定，例如可访问性和安全服务（例如实际上阻止人们大规模窃取帐户）。所有这些都可以做到，甚至很容易，但事实并非如此。政客们不想这样做。

children.author: microtonal
children.textRawHtml:
I think there are two fights that are both worth fighting:

1. Completely outlawing remote attestation.

2. In a world where remote attestation is given, let it be controlled in a fair way and not just by Google and Apple.

The risk is that only fighting for (1) leaves you in a world with remote attestation, where only Google and Apple can decide who gets to pass and who not. In fact, that is pretty much the world we are in already.

I agree that they are both worth fighting for, but I think (2) is much easier to accomplish, simply because Play Integrity is probably a DMA violation. (IANAL blah blah)
children.textZhHtml:
我认为有两场战斗都值得一战：

1.完全禁止远程认证。

2.在提供远程认证的世界中，让它以公平的方式控制，而不仅仅是由 Google 和 Apple 控制。

风险在于，仅争取 (1) 会让您进入一个具有远程认证的世界，在这个世界中，只有 Google 和 Apple 可以决定谁可以通过，谁不能通过。事实上，这几乎就是我们已经所处的世界。

我同意它们都值得为之奋斗，但我认为 (2) 更容易实现，仅仅是因为游戏完整性可能违反了 DMA。 （IANAL 等等）

children.author: altairprime
children.textRawHtml: Implementing #2 would be as simple as declaring that hardware vendors that issue attestation roots may not pre-install operating systems on them, nor make exclusivity or unfair pricing agreements that advantage or disadvantage other businesses unevenly; and, must provide a way for consumers to replace at will the operating system preinstalled by those third parties with one of their own choosing. If the EU has technical competence then they’ll work their way around to this realization and absolutely shatter the Apple/Google monopolies in a single edict, since those EU-sold devices will then be repurposeable worldwide (with valid attestation chains to the replacement OSes, if they choose to implement them). One can dream.
children.textZhHtml: 实施 #2 很简单，只需声明颁发证明根的硬件供应商不得在其上预装操作系统，也不得制定排他性或不公平的定价协议，从而对其他企业产生不均匀的优势或劣势；并且，必须提供一种方式让消费者随意更换第三方预装的操作系统。如果欧盟拥有技术能力，那么他们将努力实现这一目标，并在一项法令中彻底打破苹果/谷歌的垄断，因为这些欧盟销售的设备将可以在全球范围内重新利用（如果他们选择实施的话，可以为替代操作系统提供有效的证明链）。人可以梦想。

children.author: femboyvtuber
children.textRawHtml:
I cannot think of any company that has appropriately used attestation as a trust/risk calculation. I work in major game studio; there is never calculation only a binary.

It never „let`s check if the mobile user has purchased in-game content server side to prevent pirating it“, its „suspend any account that has signed in with a device that fails safetynet, permanently ban any account that has failed a jailbreak or root checks“

It never „let`s check and calculate statistically cheating probability and move damage calculation server-side so that player cannot godmode or modify their APK“, its „all non-stock phones are cheaters and fraudsters, ban all of them, use invasive anti-cheat, while continuing to have client sided damage and health and energy because it is easier“

Something else has to change first otherwise the only option for businsinses do will be, after 2 is implemented : „while yes it is now possible to allow a neutral third party to control attestation, someone higher-up such as legal has said ONLY google can and we will ban everyone else“

As long as it is easier to don't give a fuck, that is the option that will be taken. z.B. the only reason our publisher allow the removal of play services was finding out that chinese players on definitely not google certified phone spends the most by orders of magnitude and even then it is only relaxing the check for specific region, forcing all EU players to continue to have this checking.

I would be wholly unsurprised if the result was to continue to require attestation but allow GrapheneOS f.e. only in Motorola factory shiped phones and disallow it if the user was involved in any way in the installation of it.
children.textZhHtml:
我想不出有哪家公司适当地使用证明作为信任/风险计算。我在各大游戏工作室工作；永远不会只计算二进制文件。

它永远不会“让`s check if the mobile user has purchased in-game content server side to prevent pirating it“, its „suspend any account that has signed in with a device that fails safetynet, permanently ban any account that has failed a jailbreak or root checks“

It never „let`s检查和计算统计作弊概率并将损害计算移动到服务器端，以便玩家无法上帝模式或修改他们的APK”，它的“所有非库存手机都是作弊者和欺诈者，禁止所有这些手机，使用侵入性反作弊，同时继续产生客户端损害以及健康和能量，因为它更容易”

其他东西必须首先改变，否则这是商业的唯一选择在第 2 条实施后，将会是这样：“虽然现在可以允许中立的第三方控制认证，但法律等高层人士表示只有谷歌可以，我们将禁止其他所有人”

只要更容易不在乎，那就是我们将采取的选择。 z.B.我们的发行商允许删除游戏服务的唯一原因是发现中国玩家在绝对不是谷歌认证的手机上花费最多的数量级，即便如此，它也只是放宽了对特定地区的检查，迫使所有欧盟玩家继续进行这项检查。

如果结果是继续要求认证但允许 GrapheneOS f.e.，我完全不会感到惊讶。仅适用于摩托罗拉工厂出厂的手机，如果用户以任何方式参与安装，则不允许这样做。

children.author: qurren
children.textRawHtml:
> When two people interact there’s a trust/risk calculation on both sides.

You should never base your trust on the other party having a piece of hardware that has restrictions that you approve of. That is fragile, especially in a world where some people are better at making or modifying hardware than others. It is also a fundamental violation of basic freedoms to prevent people from modifying hardware that they own, and not something you can reliably police, and thus is a terrible way to establish trust from a technical perspective.

It's much better to base trust on established cryptographic methods on a protocol level. You treat them as a black box, and the trust is established by the inputs and outputs, not what's inside the box. An example of this would be handing them an image of a digital ID paired with a cryptographic signature that only the government holds the private keys to. They have no computationally viable way to edit the image and still have it match the paired signature. It's easily verified based on the government's public key, and they cannot re-sign it without the government's private key. It doesn't depend on hardware restrictions.

The fact that there is so much focus on hardware means there are likely deeper motives here, e.g. surveillence being dressed up as convenience.
children.textZhHtml:
> 当两个人互动时，双方都会进行信任/风险计算。

您永远不应该将信任建立在对方拥有您认可的限制的硬件上。这是脆弱的，尤其是在一个有些人比其他人更擅长制造或修改硬件的世界里。阻止人们修改自己拥有的硬件也是对基本自由的根本侵犯，而不是您可以可靠监管的东西，因此从技术角度来看，这是建立信任的糟糕方法。

最好将信任建立在协议级别上已建立的加密方法上。您将它们视为黑匣子，信任是通过输入和输出建立的，而不是通过盒子内的内容建立的。一个例子是向他们提供一个数字身份证图像，该图像与只有政府持有私钥的加密签名配对。他们没有计算上可行的方法来编辑图像并仍然使其与配对签名匹配。它可以根据政府的公钥轻松验证，并且如果没有政府的私钥，他们无法重新签名。它不依赖于硬件限制。

事实上，对硬件的如此多的关注意味着这里可能有更深层次的动机，例如监视被装扮成方便。
